items.name: accordion
items.description: A vertically stacked list of panels driven by server-authoritative open state (WIRE tier). Each panel header is a native <button> inside a heading (role="heading" aria-level), with aria-expanded and aria-controls wired to the panel region. Panel bodies use the HTML `hidden` attribute for collapsed state (removes from the a11y tree + Tab order). Open/close state is a @Wire server fact; the server validates the single/multiple mode invariant and re-renders on every toggle action. Variants: default (bordered card), ghost (dividers only), borderless (flat). No enhancer: keyboard is fully platform-supplied (native button + morph). APG Accordion pattern.

@import java.util.List @import java.util.Set @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui accordion wire template -- a vertically stacked list of panels driven by server-authoritative open state. TIER: WIRE. Open-state is a server fact (@Wire Set expandedIds); the server validates which panels are expanded, enforces single/multiple mode invariants, and re-renders this template on every toggle. The client morphs the resulting HTML; focus survives the morph on the activated header button (ADR-0019). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Accordion (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/accordion/): each panel header is a real native <%-- Panel region: hidden attribute when collapsed (removes from a11y tree + Tab order). role="region" + aria-labelledby is the APG landmark; panelRole="" suppresses it. --%>
@endfor

items.docs: WIRE accordion: open-state is server-authoritative. The AccordionComponent Java class owns the @Wire fields (panels, expandedIds, mode, collapsible) and the toggle() action. The template receives List<Map<String,String>> panels (each with id, title, optional level/disabled), Set<String> expandedIds, mode, collapsible, variant, panelRole. Wire the toggle action via l:click="toggle" on each header button (already in the template). The panel body region for each panel is an empty <div data-slot="accordion-panel"> in the shipped template; the adopter fills it with server-rendered markup in their copy of the template, keyed off the panel id. variant="default" | "ghost" | "borderless". The chevron rotation requires one adopter CSS rule: [data-slot="accordion-header"][data-expanded="true"] .lv-accordion-chevron { transform: rotate(180deg); }. No enhancer: platform native <button> + morph handle all keyboard + focus. APG Accordion pattern, WAI-ARIA compliant.

items.name: accordion
items.description: A disclosure group: several triggers each expand/collapse their own region, single or multiple open. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the open set lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-accordion> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG accordion (per-item button[aria-expanded][aria-controls] + region[aria-labelledby]).

The adopter edits the body region per item directly (this is copy-in source). That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Accordion pattern, Radix Accordion as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - each trigger is a real

Panel content for ${label}.

items.docs: Register AccordionComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `itemIds` + `labels` (index-aligned) and `mode` ('single' | 'multiple') as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.accordion(...)`. Each trigger arms its item with `l:click="$set('toggleId','<id>')"`; the @LievitRender hook flips it in the open set and clears the arm (one click, fully server-side). The panel BODY per item is OWNED markup inside the `data-accordion-region` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/accordion")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: alert-dialog
items.description: A confirm modal: an interruptive prompt requiring a response (delete this? discard?). Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012, Wave 3), the role=alertdialog specialization of the dialog wire: open-state + title/description/labels + the destructive flag live in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs; the destructive action + cancel are real l:click buttons, not slots. Renders the dialog wire's overlay/panel structure directly (no nested live instance, no <lv-dialog> island). WAI-ARIA APG alertdialog (role=alertdialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby); the backdrop does NOT dismiss (only the explicit buttons do); focus-trap + Escape are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement, open-state stays server-owned.

files.content: @import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui alert-dialog WIRE template -- a confirm modal whose open-state lives server-side in AlertDialogComponent (ADR-0012, Wave 3). Pairs with registry/wire/alert-dialog/AlertDialogComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `open` action and the destructive action + cancel buttons fire `confirm` / `cancel` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No , no : this renders the dialog wire's STRUCTURE (overlay + panel + hidden-when-closed) directly, specialized to a confirm prompt. A wire template is rendered with ONLY its own @Wire fields + _component (JteTemplateAdapter, the Wave 0 finding), so one wire component cannot nest another as a live instance; "composing the dialog" therefore means rendering its overlay/panel structure here, owned and server-rendered, which is what this template does. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Alert Dialog pattern, Radix AlertDialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit-island-backed partial cited): - the panel is role="alertdialog" + aria-modal="true" (NOT role="dialog": an alertdialog is an interruptive prompt requiring a response, so the backdrop does NOT dismiss it; the only exits are the explicit confirm / cancel buttons); - aria-labelledby -> the title id; aria-describedby -> the description id when set; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree; - the footer is two real

items.docs: Register AlertDialogComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.alert-dialog(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` from a trigger to show the prompt, `confirm` (the destructive/primary action) or `cancel` to close. Do the real destructive work server-side by overriding `confirm()` (with authz) before it closes, or read the `confirmed` wire flag on the host after the round-trip. An alertdialog is interruptive: the backdrop does NOT dismiss it, only the explicit buttons do. Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-alert-dialog-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the `cancel` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=cancel button, which is also the initial-focus element via data-lv-autofocus -- the focus-on-cancel default for an interruptive prompt), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/alert-dialog")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: alert-dialog-static
items.description: A confirmation dialog (shadcn Alert Dialog), STATIC + trigger-driven, with ZERO JS. Built on the native HTML <dialog> element like the modal partial: the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (declarative open, no script) and the browser renders it in the top layer, traps focus, and exposes it to AT. role="alertdialog" + aria-modal (WAI-ARIA APG Alert Dialog, Radix AlertDialog): an interruptive prompt that REQUIRES a response, so unlike the modal there is NO backdrop-dismiss and NO header close (x) -- the only exits are the explicit Cancel + Action footer buttons (Escape == Cancel, the recoverable platform default). Cancel is a native <form method="dialog"> submit; the Action button submits the adopter's confirm/destructive form via formaction (or closes when none is given). destructive adds a red Action button + a triangle-alert icon on the title. The static sibling of the server-state-held wire alert-dialog (registry/wire/alert-dialog/), serving the kit io.lievit.kit.component.ConfirmationModal render path. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean, no enhancer.

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui alert-dialog-static partial -- the STATIC shadcn Alert Dialog: a trigger-driven confirmation prompt with ZERO shipped JS. It is the static sibling of the WIRE alert-dialog (registry/wire/alert-dialog/): that one holds `open` + `confirmed` server-side (a @Wire boolean, the server decides + re-renders); THIS partial is the trigger-driven static case -- the open-state is the browser's native element, opened declaratively by the trigger and closed only by an explicit footer button, with no round-trip. Use the wire alert-dialog when the server owns the open/confirm flow; use this when a button on the page opens an in-page confirm whose Action posts a plain form (the kit's io.lievit.kit.component.ConfirmationModal render path). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Built on the SAME native seam as the modal partial (registry/jte/modal.jte): the browser renders it in the TOP LAYER (escapes overflow / stacking, no z-index war), traps focus inside it, paints the ::backdrop, and exposes it to AT, so the partial is CSP-clean by construction with no enhancer. ALERT DIALOG vs DIALOG (the interruptive-prompt decision rule, WAI-ARIA APG Alert Dialog, Radix AlertDialog as the a11y source): an alert dialog is an INTERRUPTIVE prompt that REQUIRES a response, so unlike the modal it has NO backdrop-dismiss and NO header close (x): the ONLY exits are the explicit Cancel / Action buttons. role="alertdialog" (not role="dialog") tells AT exactly this. Escape still cancels for free (the native modal default routes to a method="dialog" close); we accept that as the platform's interruptible escape (matching shadcn AlertDialogCancel), there is no JS-free way to forbid it without an enhancer and the prompt is recoverable (Escape == Cancel, never == confirm). SHOW/HIDE (native, zero JS), mirrored from the modal partial: - OPEN = the native invoker command API: a real

Pass a unique `id` (the commandfor target), the `trigger` content (label/icon), a `title` (wires aria-labelledby) and an optional `description` (wires aria-describedby); set `actionLabel`/`cancelLabel`, `destructive` (red Action + triangle-alert icon), and the real effect via `actionForm` (a form id) + `actionUrl` (a formaction URL) so the Action button POSTs the confirmed action with no inline JS. Tune `width` (xs|sm|md|lg|xl|2xl..7xl|full|screen, the kit Width/ModalWidth tokens). The browser owns open/close (native <dialog> showModal + Escape + focus-trap + return-focus); there is NO backdrop-dismiss and no header close (it is an interruptive prompt). For a confirm whose open-state lives server-side (the server decides open/confirmed and re-renders), use the wire alert-dialog component instead.

items.name: aspect-ratio
items.description: A CSS layout wrapper that constrains its content to a fixed width-to-height ratio using the native CSS `aspect-ratio` property (Baseline 2022). Two-element pattern: outer div handles visual framing (border-radius, border, overflow); inner div enforces the ratio. Semantically transparent: no role or aria-* on the wrapper. Purely presentational PARTIAL; no JS, no enhancer.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui aspect-ratio partial -- a CSS layout wrapper that constrains its content to a fixed width-to-height ratio. Single responsibility: maintain the declared ratio regardless of available width. Composable for responsive images, embedded videos, maps, skeleton placeholders, or any block content that must not reflow into an unwanted aspect. TIER: PARTIAL -- purely presentational CSS layout wrapper. No client state, no enhancer, no wire round-trip, no JavaScript. The content defines the accessible semantics; the wrapper is transparent. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the CSS `aspect-ratio` property is the correct modern mechanism (Baseline in all evergreen browsers since 2022; verified against caniuse.com). The legacy padding-top hack (padding-top: 56.25% + absolutely-positioned child) required non-semantic markup and extra positioning context -- rejected on ergonomics and readability. shadcn/ui AspectRatio consulted as inventory reference (wrapper + ratio mechanism); output is original generation over --lv-* tokens (no code copied). Two-element pattern (outer: visual framing -- border-radius, border, overflow clip at the correct layer; inner: ratio enforcement via aspect-ratio + size-full fill) is the idiomatic approach used by every major CSS framework for this component: it gives the adopter a clean hook for overflow control without clipping the border-radius at the wrong element. A single element with aspect-ratio on it does not allow clean overflow:hidden at the frame while keeping the content unclipped. A11y: NONE on the wrapper (negative confirmation verified against WAI-ARIA APG patterns index at https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/). This wrapper is semantically transparent: screen readers and assistive technologies see through it to the content inside. Adding any role or aria-* attribute to the wrapper would interfere with the content's own landmark, role, or name. The outer div carries NO role, NO aria-*. The inner div carries NO role, NO aria-*. Accessible semantics belong entirely to the content: - an img inside carries alt (mandatory; supplied by the adopter's content slot); - a video inside carries its own accessible controls; - an iframe inside carries title; - a skeleton inside may carry aria-busy="true" (see the loading-section spec). The outer div MUST NOT carry role="img" even when the content is an image -- the img itself carries role=img and alt; adding it to the wrapper double-announces. CREDITS: - Inventory: shadcn/ui AspectRatio used as inventory reference (wrapper + padding-top trick vs modern aspect-ratio property baseline); Ant Design has no equivalent (delegates to CSS utils). - Styling: original generation over --lv-* tokens; no code copied from any source. - A11y: negative confirmation against WAI-ARIA APG patterns index + HTML spec transparent content model concept (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/). Params: int ratioX = 16 width part of the ratio (e.g. 16 in 16:9). Typed int: type-safe, no format validation needed, template owns formatting into CSS. int ratioY = 9 height part of the ratio (e.g. 9 in 16:9). Any positive integer pair valid. String overflow = "hidden" CSS overflow on the inner content element: "hidden" | "visible" | "auto". Emitted as an inline overflow style on the outer element (matching the layer that clips the border-radius). String rounded = "" token-driven corner radius: "" (none) | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" | "full" -- mapped to --lv-radius-* via inline style on the outer element. boolean border = false renders a 1px border using --lv-color-border on the outer element. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes appended to the outer element. String innerClass = "" extra utility classes appended to the inner content element. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only (id, data-testid). Never feed user-supplied or per-row DB values through this channel. gg.jte.Content content (required) the child content: image, video, iframe, skeleton, or any block. Usage: @@template.lievit.aspect-ratio(content = @@` `) @@template.lievit.aspect-ratio(ratioX = 1, ratioY = 1, rounded = "full", cssClass = "max-w-xs", content = @@``) --%> @param int ratioX = 16 @param int ratioY = 9 @param String overflow = "hidden" @param String rounded = "" @param boolean border = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String innerClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param gg.jte.Content content <%-- Compute the CSS custom property value. Java string concatenation, not ${} inside a Java string literal (only valid in HTML template position). The ratio is authored as two typed ints so the call-site is type-safe and the partial owns the CSS formatting. --%> !{var _ratio = ratioX + " / " + ratioY;} !{var _dataRatio = ratioX + ":" + ratioY;} <%-- border-radius: mapped from rounded param to --lv-radius-* token. Empty = no radius. --%> !{var _radiusStyle = switch (rounded) { case "sm" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-sm);"; case "md" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-md);"; case "lg" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-lg);"; case "xl" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-xl);"; case "full" -> "border-radius:var(--lv-radius-full);"; default -> ""; };} <%-- border: 1px solid --lv-color-border when true; empty string otherwise. --%> !{var _borderStyle = border ? "border:1px solid var(--lv-color-border);" : "";} <%-- overflow: controls whether content clips at the outer framing element. --%> !{var _overflowStyle = "overflow:" + overflow + ";";} <%-- outer element inline style: --lv-ar-ratio CSS custom property + radius + border + overflow. --lv-ar-ratio is a per-instance runtime value (not a design-constant token in lievit-tokens.css) namespaced --lv- to stay within the library's CSS custom property namespace and to be overridable via CSS by an adopter who needs to dynamically change the ratio. --%> !{var _outerStyle = "--lv-ar-ratio:" + _ratio + ";" + _overflowStyle + _radiusStyle + _borderStyle;} <%-- inner element: enforces the numeric ratio; size-full fills the constrained box. --%> !{var _innerStyle = "aspect-ratio:var(--lv-ar-ratio);width:100%;height:100%;";}
${content}

items.docs: Copies the aspect-ratio partial to your JTE root at lievit/aspect-ratio.jte, reached as @template.lievit.aspect-ratio(content = @`...`). Default ratio is 16:9 (ratioX=16, ratioY=9). Pass typed int params for any ratio: @template.lievit.aspect-ratio(ratioX=1, ratioY=1, rounded="full", cssClass="max-w-xs", content=@``). Common ratios: 16:9 (video/hero), 4:3 (document), 1:1 (square/avatar), 3:2 (photo), 21:9 (cinematic), 9:16 (portrait/Reels). Use overflow="visible" when content inside needs to escape the clip (e.g. a tooltip anchor). Use border=true for a framing border. Add corner radius via rounded param (sm|md|lg|xl|full).

items.name: avatar
items.description: A circular (or square) frame displaying a person or entity: image, auto-derived initials, or icon fallback. Diameter-based size scale (xs/sm/md/lg/xl/2xl, architecture-contract §5.b D5 -- NOT toolbar-aligned), circle|square shape, six intent colors with deterministic auto-hash from name, optional status dot (online/away/busy/offline, suppressed at xs), interactive wrappers (<a href> or <button>), ariaHidden decorative mode. Static presentational, zero JS, CSP-clean. Params: src, name, alt, initials, icon, size, shape, color, status, href, hrefLabel, clickable, ariaLabel, ariaHidden, cssClass, attrs, dataAttrs.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui avatar/group-count partial -- the "+N" overflow tile that closes an overlapping avatar stack (shadcn new-york-v4 AvatarGroupCount, data-slot="avatar-group-count"). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The overflow count is a server fact (the server already knows how many avatars it did NOT render), so there is no client state: the whole tile is server-rendered, zero JS, CSP-clean. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn renders AvatarGroupCount as a muted, round tile the same diameter as the avatars it sits beside, carrying the same ring as the group's other tiles so it reads as a member of the overlapping stack. We reproduce that with the same muted-bg/muted-fg token pair avatar/kbd/empty use and the avatar size scale (sm/default/lg -> the same dims). A11y: the tile states the count in text (e.g. "+3"); it is not interactive and carries no role. If the count links to a "see all" view the ADOPTER wraps the call in an . Params: int count overflow count rendered as "+". String size = "default" one of: sm | default | lg (mirror the sibling avatars' size). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes (e.g. the group ring: ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]). Usage:
@@template.lievit.avatar(name = "A", initials = "A", cssClass = "ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]") @@template.lievit.avatar(name = "B", initials = "B", cssClass = "ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]") @@template.lievit.avatar.group-count(count = 3, cssClass = "ring-2 ring-[var(--lv-color-bg)]")
--%> @param int count @param String size = "default" @param String cssClass = "" !{var dim = size.equals("sm") ? "1.5rem" : size.equals("lg") ? "2.5rem" : "2rem";} +${count}

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui avatar/badge partial -- the small status dot that overlaps an avatar's corner (shadcn new-york-v4 AvatarBadge, data-slot="avatar-badge"): an online/notification indicator. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The dot's presence + colour are a server fact (the user IS online, there ARE N notifications), so no client state: server-rendered, zero JS. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): shadcn's AvatarBadge is an absolutely-positioned round tile in the avatar's bottom-right, filled with a token pair and ringed with the page surface so it reads off the photo. Because our avatar partial is overflow-hidden (it clips the photo to a circle), the badge cannot live INSIDE it; the adopter wraps the avatar in a `relative` span and drops this badge as a sibling, exactly the shadcn Root>Badge nesting expressed server-first. The fill defaults to the primary token (shadcn's bg-primary); an optional content slot carries a tiny glyph or count, else the dot is a plain status indicator. A11y: a status dot is decorative unless it carries meaning; pass a non-blank `label` to announce it (role="img" + aria-label, e.g. "Online"), else it is aria-hidden so it adds no noise. Params: String size = "default" one of: sm | default | lg (match the avatar it sits on). String label = "" accessible label (e.g. "Online"); "" => decorative (aria-hidden). gg.jte.Content content = null optional tiny glyph / count inside the dot. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes (e.g. a status fill: bg-[var(--lv-color-success)]). Usage: wrap the avatar in a relative span, drop the badge as a sibling. @@template.lievit.avatar(name = "Ada", initials = "A") @@template.lievit.avatar.badge(label = "Online", cssClass = "bg-[var(--lv-color-success)]") --%> @param String size = "default" @param String label = "" @param gg.jte.Content content = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var dim = size.equals("sm") ? "0.5rem" : size.equals("lg") ? "0.75rem" : "0.625rem";} !{var labelled = label != null && !label.isBlank();} @if(content != null)${content}@endif

items.docs: Copies avatar.jte (+ avatar/group-count.jte and avatar/badge.jte) to your JTE root. Reached as @template.lievit.avatar(name=..., src=..., size="md"). Size is diameter-based (xs=24px, sm=32px, md=40px, lg=48px, xl=64px, 2xl=80px -- NOT toolbar-aligned per architecture-contract D5). shape="circle" (default) or "square". color sets the intent background for initials/icon (primary/secondary/accent/success/warning/destructive/default); null auto-hashes from name so the same person always gets the same hue. status="online|away|busy|offline" overlays a colored dot (suppressed at xs). Set href for a link wrapper or clickable=true for a button wrapper (provide hrefLabel/ariaLabel for an accessible name). Set ariaHidden=true for a decorative avatar where adjacent text identifies the person. Dynamic per-row data goes through the SAFE dataAttrs=Map.of("user-id", row.id()) channel; static wire directives go through attrs (TRUSTED, $unsafe, author-typed only). items.name: badge
items.description: A small status pill (inert <span> or asChild <a> link) mapping each variant to a token fill + foreground pair. Two variant families side by side: the shadcn taxonomy (default, secondary, destructive, outline, ghost, link) and the status family (neutral, success, warning, danger, info). Size scale (sm/md/lg) governs text size + padding (NOT height — badge is inline, not a form control). Optional dot (coloured circle, aria-hidden) and leading slot. Pass href for an asChild <a> link (else inert <span>). Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String>, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute for per-row DB values). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 4): variant-to-colour is pure, no client state, no island. Replaces the <lv-badge> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

items.name: builder
items.description: A list of HETEROGENEOUS typed blocks (Filament's Builder): unlike the repeater (the same schema repeated), a builder mixes DIFFERENT block types in one ordered list (a page builder: heading | paragraph | image), chosen from an add-block menu. JS-OFF the add-block menu is a row of submit buttons one per type (name=`<name>__addblock`, value=type) and per-block remove is a submit (name=`<name>__remove`, value=index), both server round-trips -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer clones the matching per-type template on add and drops a card on remove, in place, then re-indexes every field name (the __type included) + label so indices stay contiguous. The native inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: labelled fieldset/legend, a labelled add-block menu of typed buttons, each card a labelled region, labelled remove, aria-live. items.name: button-group
items.description: Joins buttons (and/or nested groups, selects, input-groups) into a segmented control: a role=group flex box whose CSS collapses the shared inner rounding + borders between adjacent children (shadcn's ButtonGroup, horizontal or vertical). Pure layout over the children the caller passes in, no client state; focus-visible children raise their z-index so the ring is never clipped by a neighbour's border. TIER: PARTIAL presentational -- pure layout/styling, zero client state, zero JS. Server-first is trivial: a layout container has nothing interactive of its own. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Toolbar structural pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/toolbar/) -- button-group maps to the STRUCTURAL half: role="group" with an optional aria-label so AT announces the set as one labelled unit. items.name: calendar
items.description: A full event calendar (month/week/day) rendered server-first (ADR-0012, R1 RESOLVED). The grid, the events placed in its cells, the view, the anchor date and the filter all live in typed Java; the server renders the grid as HTML and the client morphs each re-render. The wire optimization toolkit absorbs round-trip latency: l:init/skeleton for first paint, l:loading feedback, l:model.debounce filter, l:click navigation. The one irreducible client bit -- event drag onto a day cell -- is a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer that fires the moveEvent action on drop (no Lit, no @event-calendar). Retires the <lv-calendar> Lit island and gest's ht-calendar escape-hatch. WAI-ARIA APG grid pattern.

files.content:
@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata @import io.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent @import io.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent.Cell @import io.lievit.wire.CalendarComponent.Event @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui calendar WIRE template -- a full event calendar (month/week/day) whose grid, the events placed in its cells, the view, the anchor and the filter all live SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012, R1 RESOLVED). Pairs with registry/wire/calendar/CalendarComponent.java + the calendar.ts drag enhancer. This is the single heaviest piece of the refactor: it retires both the Lit island AND gest's ht-calendar (@event-calendar) escape-hatch. There is NO Lit, NO @event-calendar, NO . TIER: WIRE. The grid is server-rendered HTML from the typed model (CalendarComponent.weeks()); the view switch + prev/next/today are l:click actions (the server re-renders the grid, the client morphs it); the filter is a @Wire string bound l:model.debounce.250ms so there is NO round-trip per keystroke. The wire OPTIMIZATION toolkit absorbs the round-trip latency a server-rendered drag-grid would otherwise pay: - Initial grid: when not yet loaded the template ships a SKELETON whose l:init="load" fires the `load` action right after bind (the wire:init parity), so first paint is immediate and the real grid arrives on the next morph; l:loading regions give instant feedback while a call resolves. - Filter/typeahead: l:model.debounce.250ms -> server re-filters a few hundred ms after the last key. - Navigation: l:click prev/next/today/viewMonth/viewWeek/viewDay re-render the resolved period. The one irreducible client bit -- event drag-resize/drag-create -- is the calendar.ts enhancer: on drop it $set's the dragged event id + the target ISO date into the wire and $call's `moveEvent`; the server owns the event store, moves the event in Java and re-renders. addEventListener only, CSP-clean. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Grid pattern for the month layout (the same model the dropped island cited): the grid is a , weekday headers are
, each day is a carrying aria-selected; today carries aria-current="date". A server-rendered grid + a tiny drop enhancer beats a client grid: the events and the layout are both server-held, so nothing can fail to project (the slot bug) and the layout cannot drift; the only client code is the irreducible drop gesture. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Grid): table[role=grid][aria-label], th[role=columnheader], td[role=gridcell] [aria-selected], the today cell aria-current="date"; the toolbar buttons carry aria-labels; the filter input is a labelled searchbox; the loading region is aria-busy while a call is in flight. Params: view String the visible view ("month" | "week" | "day"; round-trips) anchor String the ISO date the visible range is built around (round-trips) filter String the live filter query (bound by l:model.debounce.250ms) selected String the clicked day ISO (server-owned; set via $set) loaded boolean false on mount (skeleton + l:init), true once `load` has fired dragEventId String the event id the drag enhancer arms before moveEvent (round-trips) dragToDate String the ISO day the dragged event is dropped onto (round-trips) _instance CalendarComponent the live instance (reads the server-built grid + the period label) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, seed allEvents in the component from real data keyed by the visible range, register the component as a PROTOTYPE @Bean, call enhanceCalendars(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() so a day-cell drop moves the event server-side): @template.lievit.calendar(...) -- mounted by the wire pipeline. --%> @param String view @param String anchor @param String filter @param String selected @param boolean loaded @param String dragEventId @param String dragToDate @param CalendarComponent _instance @param ComponentMetadata _component !{var cid = _component.className();} !{var label = _instance.periodLabel();} !{List headers = _instance.weekdayHeaders();} !{List> weeks = _instance.weeks();}
<%-- Toolbar: period label + nav + view switch + the debounced filter. --%>
${label} <%-- l:loading region: instant feedback while any wire call for this calendar is in flight. --%> Loading…
@if(!loaded) <%-- First-paint skeleton: l:init fires `load` right after bind so the real grid arrives on the next morph; the skeleton is what the user sees instantly (initial-latency toolkit). --%>
@for(int i = 0; i < 35; i++)
@endfor
@else @for(String h : headers) @endfor @for(List week : weeks) @for(Cell cell : week) @endfor @endfor
${h}
${cell.dayOfMonth()}
@for(Event ev : cell.events()) ${ev.title()} @endfor
@endif <%-- The drag enhancer arms these over the wire ($set) before $call('moveEvent'); kept as hidden mirrors so a hand-built POST (JS off) can still move an event, and so the fields are visibly part of the owned template, not magic. --%>

items.name: chart
items.description: A server-rendered static chart as inline SVG: `type` selects bar (one <rect> per datum), line (a <polyline> through the points), area (that line closed to the baseline as a <polygon>) or pie (one <path> arc per slice). Coloured by the --lv-color-chart-N palette tokens, with axis labels and a WAI-ARIA img role (aria-label + in-SVG <title> text alternative, per-shape aria-label). Optional static legend (chart/legend.jte, data-slot="chart-legend") listing series/slices with a token swatch. Zero JS, no canvas, no client charting lib, CSP-clean. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-chart> Lit island. An interactive chart (hover tooltip, zoom) is an escape-hatch typed-TS micro-enhancement over this SVG, not shipped.

files.content:
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui chart partial -- a SERVER-rendered static chart as inline SVG (ADR-0012).
  The shapes, axis labels and accessible title are all emitted from typed @param data; there is
  NO JavaScript, NO <canvas>, NO client charting library. Replaces the dropped <lv-chart> Lit
  island, whose hover tooltip + lv-point event were the only client behaviour and are not shipped.

  TIER: PARTIAL (server-rendered SVG). A chart is a pure projection of a fixed dataset: given the
  values, the picture is fully determined server-side, so a client renderer added only the risk of
  the silent-projection bug (the exact class that motivated the pivot) for no decision the server
  could not make. The values arrive via @param from the controller's typed model and the partial
  emits declarative <svg> markup the browser draws with zero boot. INTERACTIVE charting (hover
  tooltips, zoom, live updates) is an ESCAPE-HATCH, not shipped: an adopter who truly needs it drops
  a typed-TS micro-enhancement over this SVG in their own frontend/src/ (the documented seam), the
  way gest keeps ht-calendar. lievit-ui ships no charting island.

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): each chart `type` is the one geometry whose picture is a
  trivial server computation from the typed dataset -- bar: a <rect> per datum (height = value/max);
  line: a <polyline> through the points; area: that polyline closed to the baseline as a <polygon>;
  pie: one <path> arc per datum (angle = value/total). shadcn's chart guidance and the WAI-ARIA img
  role agree: a static data graphic is a single role="img" with a text alternative, NOT a widget. So
  the partial is one <svg role="img"> with an <title>/aria-label for the accessible name, one shape
  per data point coloured by the --lv-color-chart-N tokens (the same token cascade the island used),
  and -- for the cartesian types -- a baseline + per-datum category labels under the plot. The
  optional legend (chart/legend.jte, shadcn ChartLegendContent's data-slot="chart-legend") lists the
  series/slices with a token swatch. Native SVG beats a bundled lib on every axis here: a11y (the
  title is the text alternative a screen reader reads), zero dependency, and SSR-native declarative
  markup.

  A11y (WAI-ARIA img): the <svg> carries role="img" + aria-label (the chart's accessible name) and
  an in-SVG <title> as the first child (the SVG-native text alternative). Each shape (bar / point /
  slice) also carries an aria-label="<category>: <value>" so the individual data is reachable; the
  decorative axis text + legend swatches are aria-hidden so the value is not announced twice.

  Params:
    String   label                 accessible name for the whole chart (role=img aria-label + <title>).
    String[] categories            x-axis / slice labels, one per data point.
    int[]    values                data values, aligned positionally with categories.
    String   type     = "bar"      "bar" | "line" | "area" | "pie": which geometry to render.
    String   color    = "var(--lv-color-chart-1)"  fill/stroke for the single-series types (bar/line/area).
    String   seriesName = null     legend name for the single series (bar/line/area); defaults to `label`.
    int      width    = 600        SVG viewBox width.
    int      height   = 300        SVG viewBox height.
    boolean  showAxis = true       render the category labels under the plot (cartesian types only).
    boolean  showLegend = false    render the static legend (chart/legend.jte).
    String   cssClass = ""         extra utility classes on the <svg>.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.chart(label = "Listings closed", categories = months, values = closed)
    @@template.lievit.chart(label = "Leads", categories = sources, values = leads, type = "line", color = "var(--lv-color-chart-3)")
    @@template.lievit.chart(label = "Pipeline by stage", categories = stages, values = counts, type = "pie", showLegend = true)
--%>
@param String label
@param String[] categories
@param int[] values
@param String type = "bar"
@param String color = "var(--lv-color-chart-1)"
@param String seriesName = null
@param int width = 600
@param int height = 300
@param boolean showAxis = true
@param boolean showLegend = false
@param String cssClass = ""

!{
  boolean isPie = "pie".equals(type);
  boolean isLine = "line".equals(type);
  boolean isArea = "area".equals(type);
  // the five-step --lv-color-chart-N palette (pie cycles it per slice).
  String[] palette = {
    "var(--lv-color-chart-1)", "var(--lv-color-chart-2)", "var(--lv-color-chart-3)",
    "var(--lv-color-chart-4)", "var(--lv-color-chart-5)"
  };

  int padTop = 12;
  int padRight = 12;
  int padBottom = (showAxis && !isPie) ? 28 : 12;
  int padLeft = isPie ? 12 : 36;
  int plotW = width - padLeft - padRight;
  int plotH = height - padTop - padBottom;
  int n = Math.max(values.length, 1);
  double bandW = (double) plotW / n;
  double barW = bandW * 0.7;
  int max = 1;
  for (int v : values) if (v > max) max = v;
  int baseY = padTop + plotH;

  // cartesian point coordinates (line / area), one per datum at its band centre.
  String pointsAttr = "";
  if (isLine || isArea) {
    StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
    for (int i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {
      int px = (int) Math.round(padLeft + bandW * i + bandW / 2.0);
      int py = (int) Math.round(baseY - plotH * ((double) values[i] / max));
      if (i > 0) sb.append(' ');
      sb.append(px).append(',').append(py);
    }
    pointsAttr = sb.toString();
  }
  // area closes the line back along the baseline.
  String areaPoints = "";
  if (isArea && values.length > 0) {
    int firstX = (int) Math.round(padLeft + bandW / 2.0);
    int lastX = (int) Math.round(padLeft + bandW * (values.length - 1) + bandW / 2.0);
    areaPoints = firstX + "," + baseY + " " + pointsAttr + " " + lastX + "," + baseY;
  }

  // pie geometry: total + per-slice arc path (SVG A command), centred.
  long total = 0;
  for (int v : values) total += Math.max(v, 0);
  if (total <= 0) total = 1;
  int cx = padLeft + plotW / 2;
  int cy = padTop + plotH / 2;
  int radius = Math.min(plotW, plotH) / 2;
}

<svg
  data-slot="chart"
  role="img"
  aria-label="${label}"
  viewBox="0 0 ${width} ${height}"
  preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet"
  class="block h-auto w-full ${cssClass}"
  style="font-family:var(--lv-font-sans);overflow:visible;"
>
  <title>${label}</title>

  @if(isPie)
    !{double angle = -Math.PI / 2.0;}
    @for(int i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
      !{int v = Math.max(values[i], 0);}
      !{double frac = (double) v / total;}
      !{double start = angle;}
      !{double end = angle + frac * 2.0 * Math.PI;}
      !{angle = end;}
      !{int x1 = (int) Math.round(cx + radius * Math.cos(start));}
      !{int y1 = (int) Math.round(cy + radius * Math.sin(start));}
      !{int x2 = (int) Math.round(cx + radius * Math.cos(end));}
      !{int y2 = (int) Math.round(cy + radius * Math.sin(end));}
      !{int largeArc = (end - start) > Math.PI ? 1 : 0;}
      !{String slicePath = "M " + cx + " " + cy + " L " + x1 + " " + y1 + " A " + radius + " " + radius + " 0 " + largeArc + " 1 " + x2 + " " + y2 + " Z";}
      !{String fill = palette[i % palette.length];}
      !{String cat = i < categories.length ? categories[i] : String.valueOf(i);}
      <path
        data-slot="chart-slice"
        d="${slicePath}"
        fill="${fill}"
        stroke="var(--lv-color-bg)"
        stroke-width="1"
        aria-label="${cat}: ${v}"
      ></path>
    @endfor
  @else
    <%-- baseline (cartesian types) --%>
    <line x1="${padLeft}" y1="${baseY}" x2="${width - padRight}" y2="${baseY}" stroke="var(--lv-color-border)" stroke-width="1" />

    @if(isArea)
      <polygon
        data-slot="chart-area"
        points="${areaPoints}"
        fill="${color}"
        fill-opacity="0.2"
        stroke="none"
        aria-hidden="true"
      ></polygon>
    @endif
    @if(isLine || isArea)
      <polyline
        data-slot="chart-line"
        points="${pointsAttr}"
        fill="none"
        stroke="${color}"
        stroke-width="2"
        stroke-linejoin="round"
        stroke-linecap="round"
        aria-hidden="true"
      ></polyline>
    @endif

    @for(int i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
      !{int v = values[i];}
      !{double bandX = padLeft + bandW * i;}
      !{String cat = i < categories.length ? categories[i] : String.valueOf(i);}
      @if(isLine || isArea)
        !{int px = (int) Math.round(bandX + bandW / 2.0);}
        !{int py = (int) Math.round(baseY - plotH * ((double) v / max));}
        <circle
          data-slot="chart-point"
          cx="${px}" cy="${py}" r="3"
          fill="${color}"
          aria-label="${cat}: ${v}"
        ></circle>
      @else
        !{int x = (int) Math.round(bandX + (bandW - barW) / 2.0);}
        !{int barH = (int) Math.round(plotH * ((double) v / max));}
        !{int y = baseY - barH;}
        !{int bw = (int) Math.round(barW);}
        <rect
          data-slot="chart-bar"
          x="${x}" y="${y}" width="${Math.max(bw, 1)}" height="${Math.max(barH, 0)}"
          rx="2"
          fill="${color}"
          aria-label="${cat}: ${v}"
        ></rect>
      @endif
      @if(showAxis)
        !{int labelX = (int) Math.round(bandX + bandW / 2.0);}
        <text
          x="${labelX}" y="${height - 8}"
          text-anchor="middle"
          aria-hidden="true"
          fill="var(--lv-color-muted)"
          style="font-size:11px;"
        >${cat}</text>
      @endif
    @endfor
  @endif
</svg>

@if(showLegend)
  !{String single = (seriesName != null && !seriesName.isBlank()) ? seriesName : label;}
  @if(isPie)
    !{String[] sliceColors = new String[values.length];}
    !{for (int i = 0; i < values.length; i++) sliceColors[i] = palette[i % palette.length];}
    @template.lievit.chart.legend(names = categories, colors = sliceColors)
  @else
    @template.lievit.chart.legend(names = new String[]{single}, colors = new String[]{color})
  @endif
@endif

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: chart.legend - a SERVER-rendered static legend for the chart partial. The shadcn-faithful equivalent of ChartLegendContent (data-slot="chart-legend"): one entry per series, each a coloured swatch + the series name. Here the entries are typed @param data, the swatch is a token-coloured square, and there is zero JS (no client legend, no toggle handlers). Single-series charts (bar / line / area) pass one name + one colour; the pie passes one entry per slice (the slice name + its --lv-color-chart-N token), so the legend is the slice key. A11y: the legend is decorative chrome over the chart's own role=img text alternative (every datum is already announced via the per-shape aria-label), so the swatches are aria-hidden and the list is a plain inline
    ; the names stay readable text. It adds visual key, never a second source of the data to a screen reader. Params: String[] names series / slice names, one per entry. String[] colors fill per entry, aligned positionally with names (each a --lv-color-* token). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the legend. Usage: @@template.lievit.chart.legend(names = labels, colors = palette) --%> @param String[] names @param String[] colors @param String cssClass = ""
      @for(int i = 0; i < names.length; i++) !{String swatch = i < colors.length ? colors[i] : "var(--lv-color-chart-1)";}
    • ${names[i]}
      • @endfor

items.docs: Copies the chart partial family to your JTE root: lievit/chart.jte (+ chart/legend.jte), reached as @template.lievit.chart(label=..., categories=String[], values=int[]). The controller passes the typed dataset (a String[] of x/slice labels + an int[] of values) and the partial emits a static SVG the browser draws with zero JS. Set `type` to bar (default), line, area or pie; `color` to any --lv-color-chart-N token (bar/line/area single series; pie cycles the five-step palette per slice); tune `width`/`height`, toggle `showAxis`, and set `showLegend=true` to render the static legend (chart/legend.jte). The source is yours: edit freely. If you ever need an INTERACTIVE chart (hover tooltip, zoom, live data), that is the documented escape-hatch: drop a typed-TS micro-enhancement over this SVG in your own frontend/src/ (the same seam gest uses for ht-calendar); lievit-ui ships no charting island.

items.name: checkbox
items.description: A styled native input[type=checkbox] (two-state and tri-state / indeterminate). The native element carries role=checkbox, Space/Tab activation, checked/disabled, and form-submission for free. The `sr-only peer` pattern hides the input visually while keeping it in the accessibility tree; a peer-driven aria-hidden sibling renders the custom box with an inline SVG check and dash glyph. Three sizes (sm/md/lg) use --lv-space-4/5/6 (16/20/24 px) as the box dimension; checked, focus-visible, disabled, and aria-invalid states are expressed via Tailwind peer-* utilities over --lv-* tokens. Two attribute channels: `attrs` (TRUSTED raw $unsafe -- STATIC author-typed strings such as l:model) and `dataAttrs` (SAFE escaped Map for dynamic per-row data-* values). The indeterminate DOM property is set client-side by checkbox.enhancer.ts (component-specific enhancer; coordinator registers installCheckbox in index.ts).

files.content: @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui checkbox partial -- a styled native input[type=checkbox] (two-state + tri-state). TIER: PARTIAL presentational -> JTE partial. The native checkbox carries role=checkbox, Space/Tab activation, checked/disabled semantics, and form submission for free -- there is no irreducible client state in the control itself. The CHECKED state is rendered server-side via the `checked` param; the INDETERMINATE DOM property (write-only from JS, not an HTML attribute) is set by checkbox.enhancer.ts after hydration. All other styling and ARIA is server-rendered. Server-first works naturally: server renders `checked`; the one irreducible client bit is the `el.indeterminate = true` DOM property set by the enhancer. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - shadcn's Checkbox is a Radix

items.docs: Copies the checkbox partial to your JTE root at lievit/checkbox.jte, reached as @template.lievit.checkbox(...). Renders a native input[type=checkbox] (role=checkbox, Space/Tab, form-submit for free) styled with the sr-only-peer + custom-box pattern. Pass `checked=true` for the initial checked state; pass `indeterminate=true` to render data-indeterminate="true" (the enhancer sets el.indeterminate after hydration so the browser reflects aria-checked="mixed"). Wire to a WIRE field with `attrs="l:model=\"field\""` (TRUSTED raw channel, static string). Carry a SAFE dynamic data-* (filter key, row id) via `dataAttrs=java.util.Map.of("rowid", row.id())` -- each value HTML-escaped, never through attrs. Pass `ariaLabel` when no sibling label element is associated (a11y guard). The `id` param lets a sibling `<label for>` wire to this control; omitting it auto-generates a stable id. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: checkbox-list
items.description: A multi-select FORM control: a fieldset of native <input type=checkbox> options sharing one `name`, with an optional search box and a select-all / clear bulk toggle (Filament's CheckboxList; shadcn composes Checkbox + a search). Built on the existing checkbox primitive. JS-OFF it is a fully accessible native multi-select that POSTs each checked value under the repeated `name` in a plain form (unchecked boxes are simply absent), and the search + bulk-toggle UI stays hidden so there are no dead controls; JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer reveals the tools and adds client-side filtering + a one-click select-all that fires native change so l:model and forms see it. The native checkboxes stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: native fieldset/legend group, role=searchbox on the filter, aria-pressed on the toggle. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui checkbox-list partial -- a MULTI-SELECT form control: a fieldset of native options sharing one `name`, with an optional search box and a bulk select-all / clear toggle on top (Filament's CheckboxList field; shadcn has no single equivalent, it composes Checkbox + Command/search). TIER: PARTIAL (+ optional enhancer, ADR-0012 server-first). The options, the checked state and the legend are server-rendered HTML; each option is a real native checkbox sharing the `name`, so JS-OFF the control is a fully accessible multi-select that POSTs every checked value under the same repeated `name` in a plain form (native checkbox semantics: unchecked boxes are simply absent from FormData). The search + bulk-toggle are PURE enhancements layered on top by the CSP-clean enhancer; with JS off the search/select-all UI is hidden (no dead controls) and the user simply ticks boxes. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): a custom listbox would reimplement the checkbox role, Space activation and the form POST in JS and break JS-off submit. Native checkboxes give all of that for free and POST natively, so they are the base layer; the only client bits (filter the visible options, toggle them all at once) are additive niceties, never the control itself. A11y (WAI-ARIA): a native
+ is the platform group; each option is a real +

items.name: chip
items.description: A small rounded tag, optionally removable: the Filament active-filter pill / shadcn Badge-with-dismiss. Same variant token vocabulary as badge (default/secondary/destructive/outline/ghost/link/neutral/success/warning/danger/info). Size scale (sm/md/lg) governs text size + padding (NOT height -- chip is inline, not a form control). Optional leading slot for an icon before the label. Pass content for rich children (wins over label). Pass removeHref for a trailing remove x rendered as a real <a href> (server-first removal, plain GET, no JS) and removeLabel for the remove link's aria-label (REQUIRED when removeHref is set; absence is an axe-core link-name violation). Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String>, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute for per-row DB values). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012): no client state, no island, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the chip partial to your JTE root at lievit/chip.jte, reached as @template.lievit.chip(label="..."). A generic removable tag: variant drives the fill + foreground tokens (same family as badge: default/secondary/destructive/outline/ghost/link/neutral/success/warning/danger/info); size is sm/md/lg (text + padding scale; not height-based -- chips are inline elements, not form controls). Pass label for plain text or a content slot for rich children (content wins). Pass a leading Content slot for an icon before the label. Pass removeHref to render a trailing x as a real <a href> (server-first removal, plain GET to the URL the server builds; no JS) plus removeLabel for its aria-label (REQUIRED when removeHref is set -- a missing label is an axe-core link-name violation). The template emits a dev warning comment when removeHref is set and removeLabel is absent. Extra attributes via two channels by trust: attrs is a TRUSTED raw string ($unsafe, static author-typed strings ONLY -- NEVER per-row DB values); dataAttrs is a SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes whose values are HTML-escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute. Domain meaning (which filter/entity) lives in your call, not the component. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: code-editor
items.description: A server-first code form field (Filament code-field / shadcn code parity). The canonical control is a plain monospace <textarea name=...> that POSTs its source with the surrounding <form> JS-off; a CSP-clean island progressively enhances it into a CodeMirror 6 editor (syntax highlight + line numbers + language) whose document is written back into the SAME textarea on every change, so the form keeps POSTing the field with or without JS. CodeMirror 6 is CSP-clean (no eval; theming injects a <style> element via style-mod, not inline style attributes). The CodeMirror engine + language packs are supplied by the adopter through an injectable factory so the version is pinned downstream and the island orchestration stays unit-testable.

To enhance: `npm i codemirror @codemirror/state @codemirror/view` + the `@codemirror/lang-*` packs you need, then call enhanceCodeEditors({ factory }) once from main.ts after startLievit(), passing a factory that builds an EditorView (doc, parent=element, basicSetup for line numbers + highlight, an updateListener that calls onUpdate(state.doc.toString()) on docChanged, and the language() extension chosen from the `language` arg). The factory keeps the CodeMirror version + language packs pinned in YOUR app. On submit you receive the field as raw source text. Adjust the template name freely once copied.

items.name: color-picker
items.description: A colour FORM field (Filament ColorPicker; native-first, no dep): a swatch + a native <input type=color> (the canonical no-dep colour picker) + an optional row of preset hex swatches. JS-OFF the native input carries the OS colour picker, keyboard + a11y for free and POSTs the chosen hex under `name` with zero JavaScript; the presets are a pure convenience. JS-ON a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer wires the preset <button>s to write their colour into the native input (firing native input/change so l:model + forms see it) and keeps the hex readout in sync. The native input stays the form source of truth, so the field POSTs identically whether or not JS ran. WAI-ARIA: labelled native input, aria-labelled preset buttons (colour never the only signal), aria-live hex readout. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

items.name: combobox
items.description: A searchable select with a progressive-enhancement contract (ADR-0012): JS-OFF it IS a real native <select name=...> (token-styled like native-select) that submits in a plain POST form and is fully accessible with zero JS; JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer upgrades the SAME DOM in place into a WAI-ARIA combobox (a filterable text input over a listbox built from the same <option>s) while keeping the native <select> as the form-bound source of truth, so the field POSTs the same name whether or not JS ran. The form-native counterpart to the WIRE rich-select: this ships the small/preloaded catalog eagerly and filters CLIENT-SIDE (Filament Select/BelongsToField searchable+preload), where the wire one filters SERVER-SIDE per keystroke (lazy, needs a live connection, no JS-off render). Same options contract as native-select (List / optionGroups / content slot). `multiple` makes the native base a <select multiple> + renders removable chips JS-on. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens; the only client bit is in-page filter + listbox keyboard nav, a typed-TS module the strict CSP accepts. Binds via l:model on the native <select>.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui combobox partial -- a SEARCHABLE select rendered server-side with a progressive- enhancement contract (ADR-0012). JS-OFF it IS a real native is kept in the DOM as the form-bound source of truth (the listbox writes the chosen value back to it), so the field stays form-associated and POSTs the same name whether or not JS ran. This is the form-native counterpart to the WIRE rich-select (registry/wire/rich-select): the WIRE one filters SERVER-SIDE per keystroke (lazy / huge catalogs, l:model.debounce, needs a live connection, no JS-off render). THIS one ships the whole (small/preloaded) catalog eagerly so the filtering is client-side and the JS-off path is a working native control. Pick this for the Filament "Select / BelongsToField searchable + preload" case; pick the wire one for lazy search. TIER: PARTIAL (+ enhancer). The catalog and the selected value are server-rendered HTML; the only client bit is the in-page filtering + listbox keyboard navigation, a small typed-TS module the strict CSP accepts (no inline on* handlers; the enhancer attaches listeners in code). There is nothing for the server to DECIDE between keystrokes when the catalog is already on the page, so a per-keystroke wire round-trip would be chat for no gain -- the value is bound at POST. SHAPE DECISION (scientific rule, same lens as native-select): a custom listbox reimplements keyboard + focus + AT semantics in JS and breaks no-JS form submit; the native as the base layer (a11y + bundle + no-JS submit all win) and ADD the searchable listbox only as an enhancement on top, never a replacement. Mirrors shadcn's Combobox (Popover + Command listbox) but with a native (POSTs repeated `name` values JS-off); JS-on the enhancer renders the chosen options as removable chips above the search and keeps aria-multiselectable on the listbox. The native multi-select stays the source of truth. items.name: data-table
items.description: A server-sorted, server-paginated data table: a real <table> whose sort and page are SERVER facts (sortable headers are real <a href> re-sort links carrying aria-sort, paging via the pagination partial). Rows come in via a Content slot (caller owns the cells). Empty state. Plus the shadcn data-table feature set as server-first slots: a leading checkbox column (select-all + per-row, setting data-state=selected), a toolbar region (search/filter + column-visibility dropdown), and a selected-count footer with a bulk-action slot. Zero JS, zero client sort, CSP-clean. The presentational primitive the kit's admin list composes.

@import gg.jte.Content @import java.util.List <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table (static JTE partial - server-sorted/paginated table) The SERVER-FIRST data table: a real whose sort and page are SERVER facts. Sortable column headers are real re-sort links (the server produces the next sort URL per column); clicking one re-sorts on the server and re-renders. Pagination is the windowed pagination partial (real page links). The empty state renders a single spanning row when there are no rows. ZERO client JavaScript, ZERO client sort. TIER (why this is a partial, not a Lit island / wire component): the genuinely STATEFUL server-sorted/paginated table is OWNED by lievit-kit's admin list -- the ListingListComponent wire holds the page/sort/search/filter as @Wire fields, AdminListView.of(...) reads one bounded page, and l:click="toggleSort/prev/next" actions re-render it. This partial is NOT that engine; it is the presentational PRIMITIVE the kit (and any plain Spring controller) composes to RENDER one page. The kit's listing-list.jte hand-rolls this exact
///pagination markup; this partial generalises it so the markup, the aria-sort contract, and the sort-link affordance live in one owned place instead of being copy-pasted per resource. The old Lit island (client sort + client pagination, fully in the browser) is removed by ADR-0012: the table is server-first, the kit holds the state, this partial draws it. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule). shadcn's Data Table is a TanStack-Table client grid (sort / paginate / select all in JS); the WAI-ARIA APG "sortable table" pattern is a plain
whose sorted
carries aria-sort and whose header is an in-cell control that re-sorts. For a server-first kit the APG sortable-table pattern wins on (1) a11y (real table semantics, the sort control is a real link that works with JS off) and (5) robustness (the sort and page survive a reload because they are URL state, not in-memory client state). shadcn's grid is rejected: it puts the sort/page truth in the browser, the exact silent-client-state class the pivot removes. So: a real // (composing the table primitive's look),
headers, aria-sort on the active column, the sort affordance as a real (the kit emits the next-sort URL), and the pagination partial for paging. ROWS via a Content slot (data down, the caller owns the cells). The caller renders one @template.lievit.data-table.row(...) per data item inside `rows`, each holding @template.lievit.data-table.cell blocks (or composes the existing table.row / table.cell primitives). This mirrors the kit's typed Cell switch: the partial owns the chrome + the sort/page contract, the caller owns what a cell shows (a badge, a link, an icon, escaped text). No row data is hardcoded here; it arrives via the slot from the controller's typed model. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG, sortable table): real /// rows (caller owns them; one row per data item). int rowCount = -1 number of data rows; <= 0 renders the empty state. -1 = unknown (trust the slot; never render the empty row). String emptyText = "No data" text of the empty-state row. String caption = null optional
; the active sort column's carries aria-sort="ascending|descending" (others "none"); the sort control is a real so it is keyboard reachable and works with JS off; a visible sort indicator glyph (chevron-up/down for asc/desc, chevrons-up-down for unsorted) is aria-hidden (the aria-sort state is the accessible signal); the optional
is the table's accessible name; the empty state is one row spanning all columns. The pagination partial owns its own nav landmark + aria-current. Params: List headers column header labels, in order (REQUIRED). List sortUrls = null per-column next-sort URL; "" / null = that column is not sortable (no link). Same length / order as `headers`. List sortStates = null per-column active sort: "asc" | "desc" | "" (none). Same length / order as `headers`. null = nothing sorted. Content rows the (accessible name). String ariaLabel = null aria-label when there is no visible caption. int page = 0 current page (1-based); a page < 1 hides the pager. int totalPages = 0 total pages; <= 1 hides the pager. String pageHrefPattern = null printf pattern for a page URL (one %d = the page number); null hides the pager. Forwarded to @template.lievit.pagination. String klass = "" extra classes on the . Usage: @@template.lievit.data-table( headers = view.headers(), sortUrls = sortUrls, // controller builds the next-sort href per column sortStates= sortStates, // "asc"/"desc"/"" per column from the active Sort caption = "Listings", rowCount = view.rows().size(), page = view.pagination().page(), totalPages= view.pagination().totalPages(), pageHrefPattern = "/admin/listings?page=%d", rows = @@` @@for(var row : view.rows()) @@template.lievit.data-table.row(content = @@` @@template.lievit.data-table.cell(content = @@`${row.id()}`) `) @@endfor `) --%> @param java.util.List headers @param java.util.List sortUrls = null @param java.util.List sortStates = null @param gg.jte.Content rows @param int rowCount = -1 @param String emptyText = "No data" @param String caption = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param int page = 0 @param int totalPages = 0 @param String pageHrefPattern = null @param String klass = "" !{var colCount = headers.size();} !{var showPager = pageHrefPattern != null && page >= 1 && totalPages > 1;}
@if(caption != null && !caption.isBlank()) @endif @for(int i = 0; i < colCount; i++) !{var label = headers.get(i);} !{var url = (sortUrls != null && i < sortUrls.size()) ? sortUrls.get(i) : null;} !{var sortable = url != null && !url.isBlank();} !{var state = (sortStates != null && i < sortStates.size()) ? sortStates.get(i) : "";} !{var sorted = state != null && (state.equals("asc") || state.equals("desc"));} !{var ariaSort = !sortable ? null : (state.equals("asc") ? "ascending" : (state.equals("desc") ? "descending" : "none"));} @endfor @if(rowCount == 0) @else ${rows} @endif
${caption}
@if(sortable) ${label} @if(state.equals("asc")) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-up", size = "0.85em") @elseif(state.equals("desc")) @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down", size = "0.85em") @else @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevrons-up-down", size = "0.85em") @endif @else ${label} @endif
${emptyText}
@if(showPager) <%-- pageHrefPattern is a printf "...?page=%d"; v-next pagination takes a baseUrl prefix and appends "?page=N" itself, so strip the canonical page-query suffix to the base. --%> !{var pageBaseUrl = pageHrefPattern == null ? null : pageHrefPattern.replace("?page=%d", "").replace("&page=%d", "");}

@endif

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui :: data-table.selection-cell - the leading checkbox column of the server-first data table. shadcn's Data Table prepends a checkbox column (id="select"): a select-all checkbox in the header (checked when the whole page is selected, indeterminate when some are), and a per-row checkbox that toggles row.getIsSelected() -- the same boolean the row's data-state="selected" tint already reads. This partial renders THAT column's one cell: a header cell () for the select-all box, or a body cell () for a per-row box. The checkbox itself is the lievit checkbox partial (native , JS-off submit, indeterminate via aria-checked="mixed"). The partial renders the STRUCTURE + a wire hook; the adopter / lievit-kit wires the toggle. Pass `model` for an l:model binding (the checkbox partial binds it to the native change event, the documented checkbox wire idiom). With JS off the checkbox still POSTs its `value` under `name`, so a plain form-based bulk action (a surrounding
+ submit) works unchanged. The checkbox-column spacing rule (shadcn table.tsx): a cell that HOLDS a [role=checkbox] drops its right padding (`[&:has([role=checkbox])]:pr-0`) so the box sits flush; this partial bakes pr-0 in. A11y: the select-all box gets aria-label "Select all" (no visible label fits a header checkbox); a row box gets aria-label "Select row". `indeterminate` renders the tri-state "mixed" glyph for the "some rows selected" header state. "Select all" : "Select row");} !{var modelAttr = (model != null && !model.isBlank()) ? @import gg.jte.Content
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui description-list/item partial -- one label->value row of a description-list: a <dt>
  term + a <dd> definition, stacked. The unit the kit's AdminViewView.Section entries
  (label -> display string) and Filament's infolist entry-wrapper map onto.

  TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout; `term` is plain text (a humanized
  attribute label), `content` is the already-projected value markup (a string, a badge, a swatch).

  STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the semantic pair is <dt> (term) + <dd> (definition); both
  must be direct children of the <dl> for the native association, so this partial emits them as a
  sibling pair (NO wrapping element between the <dl> and its dt/dd). The term sits above the value
  (Filament's stacked entry-wrapper); use the parent description-list `columns` for the multi-column
  grid. An entry may span more columns via columnSpan (the kit Entry.columnSpan), applied to BOTH
  the dt and dd so the pair stays a grid cell-pair.

  Text size is NOT set on dt/dd: it is inherited from the parent <dl> container, which drives it
  via `size` (sm/md/lg). This keeps the pair's text density consistent with the container.

  colon: when true (default) appends ":" to the rendered term text so authors write plain labels
  ("Nome") and the colon is a formatting concern, not data. Set false for contexts that supply
  their own punctuation or where a colon is semantically wrong.

  A11y: native <dt>/<dd> association; the term labels the value with no extra ARIA. `content` may be
  an infolist-entry (a badge / swatch / icon), which carries its own semantics.

  Params:
    String term                   the label (a humanized attribute name).
    gg.jte.Content content        the value markup (string, badge, infolist-entry...).
    int columnSpan  = 1           parent grid columns this pair spans (the kit Entry.columnSpan).
    boolean colon   = true        append ":" to the term text; set false to suppress.
    String cssClass = ""          extra utility classes on the <dd> value.

  Usage:
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Email", content = @@`mario@@example.com`)
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Stato", content = @@`@@template.lievit.badge(label = "Attivo", variant = "success")`)
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Note", columnSpan = 2, content = @@`Cliente storico.`)
    @@template.lievit.description-list.item(term = "Nome", colon = false, content = @@`Mario Rossi`)
--%>
@param String term
@param gg.jte.Content content
@param int columnSpan = 1
@param boolean colon = true
@param String cssClass = ""

!{var span = Math.max(1, columnSpan);}
!{var spanStyle = span > 1 ? "grid-column:span " + span + " / span " + span + ";" : "";}
!{var termText = colon ? term + ":" : term;}

<dt
  data-slot="description-list-term"
  class="font-[var(--lv-font-medium)] text-[var(--lv-color-muted-fg)]"
  style="${spanStyle}"
>${termText}</dt>
<dd
  data-slot="description-list-value"
  class="m-0 mb-[var(--lv-space-1)] font-[var(--lv-font-normal)] text-[var(--lv-color-fg)] ${cssClass}"
  style="${spanStyle}"
>${content}</dd>

@import io.lievit.component.ComponentMetadata <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui dialog WIRE template -- an accessible modal dialog whose open state lives server-side in DialogComponent (ADR-0012). Pairs with registry/wire/dialog/DialogComponent.java. TIER: WIRE. The open/closed state is server state (a @Wire boolean); the trigger fires the `open` action and the backdrop / close button fire `close` over the wire; the server re-renders this template and the client morphs it. No (the light-DOM slot bug). The wire runtime renders this template with ONLY the component's @Wire fields + _component + _instance (JteTemplateAdapter), so the body + footer are NOT JTE Content slots supplied at call time: they are OWNED markup inside this region. The adopter edits the body + footer regions directly (this is copy-in source), or drives them from @Wire fields / the _instance. That is the server-first equivalent of "children": owned, server-rendered, cannot fail to project. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (Modal) pattern, Radix Dialog as the a11y source (the same source the dropped Lit island cited): - the panel is role="dialog" + aria-modal="true"; - aria-labelledby -> the heading id when a heading is set, else aria-label="Dialog"; - aria-describedby -> the description id when a description is set; - when closed the whole overlay is `hidden`, so it leaves the tab order + a11y tree. Open-state is SERVER (wire); focus-trap + Escape are a tiny CSP-clean typed-TS enhancement (data-lv-dialog hooks): zero open-state in the client, only the focus mechanics. Escape + the backdrop dispatch the `close` action; the close button does too. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Dialog/Modal): div[role=dialog][aria-modal=true] labelled by the heading + described by the description; closed overlay removed from the a11y tree via `hidden`. Params: open boolean modal open state (server-owned) heading String dialog title; wired to aria-labelledby (empty hides the header) description String supporting line; wired to aria-describedby (empty omits it) dismissible boolean when true the backdrop + Escape route to `close` (locked server-side) _component ComponentMetadata always-present wire metadata (the className anchor) Usage (copy this template in, then put YOUR body + footer markup in the owned regions):

<%-- Backdrop: a sibling button covering the overlay so a click on the empty area closes (dismissible). Keeping it a real button keeps the close path CSP-clean + keyboard-safe. --%> @if(dismissible) @endif
@if(hasHeading)

${heading}

@endif

items.docs: Register DialogComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.dialog(...)`. The open state is server-owned: fire `open` to show it and `close` (backdrop / Escape / close button) to hide it. Put your dialog body + footer markup directly in the data-lv-dialog-body / data-lv-dialog-footer regions of the copied template (they are owned source, NOT slots). Focus-trap + Escape + return-focus ship as the shared CSP-clean overlay.enhancer.ts (one module for dialog/sheet/drawer/alert-dialog): import it once in your entry module and call enhanceAllOverlays() on load and after any DOM swap. It traps Tab within [data-lv-dialog-panel] while the overlay is shown, routes Escape to the server `close` path (it clicks the rendered l:click=close button, so Escape and the button take the same wire path), and returns focus to the trigger on close. The open-state itself stays server (wire). The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/dialog")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: empty
items.description:
A full-width blank-slate placeholder for surfaces with no data (empty list, empty search, empty inbox). Composes an illustration (default Lucide icon per variant, a custom imageUrl, or a caller-owned image Content slot) + optional title

+ optional description

+ optional action Content slot (CTA). Four variants drive the default icon: default (inbox), search (search-x), error (triangle-alert), offline (wifi-off). Three sizes scale the illustration, title, and description proportionally: sm (fits inside a combobox listbox), md (default, inline section), lg (full-page blank-slate). iconOnly=true suppresses text + action (canonical for sm inside small containers). No hardcoded copy: title=null omits the title element; the controller supplies all strings from its own message source. Purely presentational PARTIAL (ADR-0012): no client state, no enhancer, no wire action. Extra attributes via two escaping channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, $unsafe, STATIC author strings only such as aria-label or id) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map of String to String, each value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute). The custom imageUrl is also safe-escaped before emission as an src attribute value.
items.docs: Copies the empty partial to your JTE root at lievit/empty.jte, reached as @template.lievit.empty(). Call with no params for a zero-config placeholder. Pass variant to pick the default icon intent (default/search/error/offline). Pass size to scale the illustration (sm/md/lg). Pass title and description as strings from your controller's message source -- the partial never bakes in default copy. Pass imageUrl for a custom illustration URL (safe-escaped as an HTML attribute). Pass imageAlt with a non-empty string to mark the image as semantic (otherwise aria-hidden decorative treatment applies). Pass iconOnly=true to suppress all text and action (canonical empty state inside a combobox dropdown or a small table cell). Pass an action Content slot for a CTA button or link. Pass an image Content slot to fully replace the illustration layer with your own markup. Extra attributes go through two channels: attrs is TRUSTED raw ($unsafe, static author-typed strings only -- aria-label, id, data-testid, wire directives -- NEVER per-row DB values); dataAttrs is SAFE (Map of String to String, each value HTML-escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute, the only safe channel for dynamic data). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: file-upload
items.description: A server-rendered drag-and-drop dropzone + file list whose upload runs over the wire upload path: a native <input type=file l:upload> POSTs the bytes out-of-band, the endpoint returns a signed temp-file reference, and the wire sets the server-held file list (the bytes never ride the snapshot). Remove drops a file from the server-held list. A CSP-clean island progressively enhances the dropzone with drag-and-drop, local thumbnail previews + progress, remove and reorder, driven by the runtime upload events (the temp-upload wire protocol seam); JS-off the native multiple <input type=file> still POSTs. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-file-upload> Lit island. WAI-ARIA dropzone-as-button + labelled file list. The runtime upload directive (l:upload) is ready (runtime/features/uploads.ts).
