@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui form partial (v-next) -- the server-first Form: a native
wrapper that owns a form-level validation summary region and stacks its field children. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. No client state: validation runs server-side, the controller passes `errors` (form-level list) or `error` (single back-compat string) down, and this partial does the aria wiring. JS-off the form POSTs natively; with the wire runtime present the same markup submits over the wire. V-NEXT CHANGES vs Wave-1b form: - `errors` list param: `java.util.List>` where each map carries "fieldId" (nullable, for anchor links) and "message" keys. Replaces the old single `error` string for the v-next error summary pattern. - `errorSummaryHeading`: customisable heading inside the error summary block. - `focusOnError`: adds `data-lv-autofocus` to the summary when there are errors, so an enhancer (e.g. the wire submit interceptor) can auto-focus it on failed submit. - `layout`: "stacked" | "inline" | "grid" (v-next form-level layout). - `labelWidth`: CSS value for `--lv-form-label-width` (passed to fields via CSS cascade). - `size`: propagated via `--lv-form-size` custom property for fields to inherit. - `autocomplete`, `enctype`, `novalidate`, `headingId`, `ariaLabel` HTML attributes. - `footer` slot: rendered below the content in a `data-slot="form-footer"` wrapper. - Error summary is ALWAYS in DOM (aria-live pre-registered); `hidden` attr gates visibility. BACK-COMPAT LAYER (immutable tests pin these contracts): - `error` (single string) back-compat path preserved; maps to a single-entry error list internally when the v-next `errors` list is empty/null. - `data-slot="form-error"` on the error region (jte-static-partials.test.ts pins this). - `role="alert"` + `aria-live="assertive"` on the error region. - `id="${errorId}"` derived from `id` param. - `aria-describedby="${hasError ? errorId : null}"` on the . - `${content}` must appear in the markup. - `method="${method}"` on the . STRUCTURE:
[heading when non-empty] [error list with fieldId anchor links or plain text]
${content} [
${footer}
when footer != null] A11y: - Native
landmark: no extra role needed. - Error summary: `role="alert"` carries implicit aria-live="assertive" aria-atomic="true" per the ARIA spec (WAI-ARIA APG Alert pattern). `aria-live="assertive"` is also explicit for engine compatibility. The summary is always in the DOM so AT registers it early. - `hidden` attribute gates visibility without removing the element (preserves aria-live registration on page load; the AT hears the update when `hidden` is removed). - `focusOnError` + `data-lv-autofocus`: a JS enhancer focuses the summary after a failed submit, so keyboard/SR users land on the error list immediately. - `novalidate`: suppresses native browser validation UI (server validates; native popups disrupt the custom error pattern). Default true per WAI-ARIA form best practice. - `aria-describedby` on the form references the error summary while errors are present (back-compat; removed when no errors to avoid spurious SR description). items.name: hover-card
items.description: A hover/focus-triggered preview card, CSS-only (Radix preview model: no ARIA role, aria-hidden, not focus-trapping). Anchors below the trigger with align (start/center/end) + sideOffset. Content lives in the DOM, revealed by group-hover/group-focus-within. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui hover-card partial -- a hover/focus-triggered preview card, CSS-only (no JS). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The former island used Floating UI + open/close timers + Lit state. Per ADR-0012 the server-pure path is CSS: the panel content is ALREADY in the DOM and is revealed by the group's :hover / :focus-within, with no client state and no positioning JS. The open/close delay nuance is dropped deliberately (a delay needs a timer = client state); the gap between trigger and panel is kept crossable by laying the panel directly under the trigger with a small token offset. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): Radix's hover-card MODEL (preview affordance, not an announced popup) rendered with a CSS group-hover reveal. Tailwind's group + group-hover / group-focus-within utilities express "show the panel when the trigger area is hovered or focused" without any script -- the canonical CSS-only disclosure. A preview card sits in normal flow under its trigger (side=bottom, the Radix default); no CSS Anchor Positioning needed. POSITIONING (server-first equivalent of Radix align/sideOffset): shadcn places HoverCardContent with align=center default + sideOffset=4. We map `align` (start/center/end) to the horizontal anchoring under the trigger and `sideOffset` to the token gap below it, computed at render time; `data-align` mirrors Radix's data attribute so a stylesheet can hook it. side stays bottom (a preview reads below its trigger); flip it in your own copy if a layout needs it. A11y (Radix preview model): the trigger is a normal interactive element; the panel carries NO ARIA role and is aria-hidden="true" -- it is a sighted-pointer/keyboard preview, so the same information MUST also be reachable in the page flow for assistive tech. Not focus-trapping. Params: gg.jte.Content trigger the trigger element (a link / button / text). gg.jte.Content content the preview body, revealed on hover/focus. String align = "center" one of: start | center | end (horizontal anchoring). String sideOffset = null the gap below the trigger (a token; defaults to the level-1 spacing token when null). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wrapper. Usage: @@template.lievit.hover-card( trigger = @@`@@ada`, content = @@`

Ada Lovelace, first programmer.

items.name: icon
items.description: Inline Lucide <svg> by name, sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens. Zero JS, CSP-clean. The root PARTIAL: every partial/wire template that needs a glyph composes it via @template.lievit.icon(name=...).

files.content:
@import static io.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body
<%--
  Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta
  Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License").

  lievit-ui icon partial -- renders an inline Lucide <svg> by name.

  DELIVERY DECISION (scientific, issue #428): inline-per-name, NOT an SVG sprite.
  Compared against MUI (font/SVG components), Radix (per-icon SVG components), shadcn
  (lucide-react, per-icon, tree-shaken) and Web Awesome (<wa-icon>, font/registry):
    (1) tree-shakeability -- inline-per-name wins: only the icons vendored into
        registry/icons/ exist in the body map, so an adopter ships exactly what they use.
        A sprite ships the whole <symbol> sheet (or needs a build to assemble a per-page
        subset) and an extra request / external <use href> with its CSP+cross-doc quirks.
    (2) ergonomics for a JTE adopter -- "add an icon" = drop a .svg in registry/icons/ and
        re-run generate-icon-map.mjs. No sheet to maintain, no <use> id registry.
    (3) robustness -- no <use href>, no external ref, CSP-clean, zero JS.
  The sprite's only edge (repeated-icon byte savings) is marginal in a gestionale and costs
  the copy-in simplicity, so inline-per-name is objectively best here.

  The uniform <svg> wrapper lives HERE once (sized + coloured by --lv-* tokens); the per-icon
  inner body (paths/circles/...) comes from io.lievit.ui.LievitIcons.body(name) -- a lievit-OWNED
  facade over an IconResolver SPI, defaulting to the bundled, tree-shaken Lucide set
  (LucideIconResolver). The partial names NO adopter class, so it renders standalone out of the box.
  To ship a different icon set, implement io.lievit.ui.IconResolver and call
  LievitIcons.setResolver(...) once at startup; this call site never changes. The Lucide SVGs
  stroke with currentColor, so colour follows the surrounding text colour or any Tailwind `text-*`
  utility / --lv-color-* override on a wrapping element.

  Usage:
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "chevron-down")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "trash-2", cssClass = "text-[var(--lv-color-danger)]")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "search", size = "1.25rem")
    @template.lievit.icon(name = "settings", label = "Open settings")   // labelled (img role)

  By default the icon is decorative: aria-hidden="true" + focusable="false". Pass a `label`
  to expose it to assistive tech (role="img" + aria-label); then it is NOT aria-hidden.

  Add an icon: drop registry/icons/<name>.svg (any Lucide SVG), run
  `node registry/icons/generate-icon-map.mjs`, commit the regenerated map.
--%>
@param String name
@param String size = "var(--lv-icon-size)"
@param String cssClass = ""
@param String label = null
<svg
  xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  viewBox="0 0 24 24"
  width="${size}"
  height="${size}"
  fill="none"
  stroke="currentColor"
  stroke-width="var(--lv-icon-stroke)"
  stroke-linecap="round"
  stroke-linejoin="round"
  class="lv-icon ${cssClass}"
  role="${label == null ? null : "img"}"
  aria-hidden="${label == null ? "true" : null}"
  aria-label="${label}"
  focusable="false"
  data-lucide="${name}"
>$unsafe{body(name)}</svg>

items.name: infolist-entry
items.description: A single labelled key-value row (<dt>+<dd>) used inside a description list (<dl>). Renders a read-only fact with a label and a value region — the infolist / descriptions pattern for a record detail view (name, status, address, date). Five variants (default/highlight/destructive/success/warning) control the value ink + tinted background. Three sizes (sm/md/lg) control label font, value font, and block padding-y. Orientation: vertical (label stacked above value) or horizontal (label column left, responsive collapse). Bordered mode adds a --lv-color-border cell ring. Colon after label via CSS ::after (not in DOM; screen-reader safe). Colspan emitted as data-colspan for the parent CSS grid. Optional leading slot for an icon or avatar in <dt>. Optional value slot (gg.jte.Content) for any server-rendered markup; absent -> empty fallback string. Loading state: skeleton pulse + aria-busy. Copyable: native <button> wired via data-lievit-action='copy' runtime directive (Clipboard API, CSP-clean). Safe escaping via two channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw, static strings) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map, Escape.htmlAttribute). v-next re-forge of the kit-facing display row: replaces the old type-switch (color/icon/image/code) with the spec-conformant label+value+slot API.
items.docs: Copies the infolist-entry partial to your JTE root at lievit/infolist-entry.jte, reached as @template.lievit.infolist-entry(label="...", value=...). Place it inside a <dl> container; the partial renders one <dt>+<dd> pair per call. Pick a variant (default/highlight/destructive/success/warning) to colour the value region. Use the leading slot for an icon or avatar in the label. Pass value=null or omit it to show the empty fallback (default '—', overridable via the empty param). Set loading=true for a skeleton pulse while the parent WIRE fetches. Set copyable=true for a runtime-wired copy-to-clipboard button (uses the native Clipboard API via data-lievit-action='copy'). Set colspan=N for entries that span multiple grid columns in a CSS-grid <dl>. Set orientation='horizontal' for a side-by-side label+value layout (label column width controlled by --lv-infolist-label-w, default 8rem). Extra attributes via two channels: attrs (TRUSTED raw $unsafe, static author-typed strings only) and dataAttrs (SAFE Map<String,String> of dynamic data-* attributes, each value HTML-escaped). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: input
items.description: Foundational single-line text input partial. A native <input> inside a styled flex-row container that supports leading/trailing adornment slots, optional clearable button, optional live character-count badge, four semantic states (default/focused/invalid/disabled), three toolbar-aligned sizes (sm/md/lg), and a borderless layout variant for inline-edit surfaces. The native element supplies role=textbox, keyboard, and focus for free; no JS is needed unless showCount or clearable are enabled (input-count.enhancer.ts). Binds via l:model or a plain form name; server-renders the current value into value='' with no client round-trip.

files.content: @import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input partial -- the foundational single-line text input control. TIER: PARTIAL (+ENH for showCount/clearable). Pure presentation: the native owns role=textbox, keyboard interaction, focus management, and form binding for free. The only irreducible client behaviour (live count, clearable button visibility) is handled by input-count.enhancer.ts; all other wiring (l:model, l:keydown.enter) passes through attrs. Server-first works trivially: the server renders the current value into value="${...}" and the user sees it immediately with no client round-trip. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA 1.2 textbox role: https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-1.2/#textbox (native element mapping; no APG pattern page for textbox -- the platform supplies role, keyboard, and focus). - APG "Providing Accessible Names and Descriptions": https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/practices/names-and-descriptions/ (labeling rule). - Inventory: Ant Design Input (prefix/suffix adornments, clearable, character count, status, size, borderless); the search-compound composes this partial inside input-group. - Styling: ORIGINAL generation over --lv-* (OKLCH) tokens; look inspired by Tailwind UI. - The outer container is a flex row; the native flexes to fill; leading/trailing zones receive adornment slots. The container carries :focus-within for the focus ring so the ring wraps the entire compound control, not just the bare input. A11y (WAI-ARIA 1.2 textbox, platform-native): - The native implicitly carries role=textbox (+ specialised roles for type=search etc.); do NOT add a redundant role="textbox" attribute. - aria-invalid="true" when invalid=true. - aria-readonly="true" when readonly=true (native readonly also present). - aria-required="true" when required=true (native required also present). - aria-disabled NOT emitted: native disabled already removes the element from the tab order and the accessibility tree's interactive node set. - aria-placeholder NOT emitted: the native placeholder maps to aria-placeholder automatically. - Labeling: a visible

The native <input> POSTs under name= with zero JS; bind to a wire field with attrs='l:model="field"'. size= (sm/md/lg) is height-based so the input lines up flush with a button or native-select of the same size in a toolbar row. The enhancer (input-count.enhancer.ts) is optional: fields without showCount or clearable need no JS. Call enhanceAllInputs() on DOMContentLoaded and after WIRE morphs.

items.name: input-group
items.description: A fused-border group wrapper that composes one CORE form control (input, native-select, or any element) with optional leading/trailing addons and peer elements into one visually cohesive unit. The group is purely presentational: layout shell + shared border + focus-within ring; all state lives in the inner controls. Slots: content (required, the core control), leadingElement / trailingElement (full-peer controls with shared border), leadingAddon / trailingAddon (decorative or lightweight-interactive pieces on muted background). size is height-based (sm/md/lg) so the group aligns flush with buttons and inputs in a toolbar row. role="group" is emitted only when ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy is set (WAI-ARIA grouping rule). Invalid state: aria-invalid + destructive ring. No enhancer, no client state.

@import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-group partial -- a fused-border group wrapper that composes one CORE form control (input, native-select, or any element) with optional leading/trailing addons and peer elements into one visually cohesive unit. The group is PURELY presentational: all state and value management lives in the inner controls; the group provides only the layout shell, the shared border frame, and the focus-within ring. TIER: PARTIAL -- pure server-rendered presentation. No enhancer, no client state, no data-lievit-component. The consuming template places the core control in the `content` slot and optional addons/elements in the four flanking slots; the server renders the HTML in one pass. This is the simplest non-trivial layout primitive in the library. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): - WAI-ARIA spec role="group" (https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-1.2/#group): a named group is emitted ONLY when ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy is set -- a gratuitous role="group" without an accessible name is a WAI-ARIA error. When the inner control has its own label (via the surrounding field partial) the root is a plain
(no role). - APG Grouping + Landmark guidance: the "group" role marks a cluster as a named unit; reserve it for compound groups where a shared label genuinely adds information (e.g. "Date range"). - Inventory reference: Ant Design Input.Group + Input affix model (prefix/suffix text, icon prefix/suffix, before/after select addons) -- slot semantics adapted, no code copied. - Styling: ORIGINAL generation over --lv-* tokens; visual look inspired by Tailwind UI Input with addons (fused borders, shared radius, muted addon background) -- no code copied. ELEMENT vs ADDON (the two slot kinds): - leadingElement / trailingElement: a full-peer control (select, button) that shares the group border. Gets its own --lv-color-input background; separated from the core control by a 1px internal divider (border-r / border-l at --lv-color-border). Receives the group's outer border-radius on the outer side (rounded-l-* for leading, rounded-r-* for trailing). - leadingAddon / trailingAddon: a decoration or lightweight interactive piece (text label, icon, small icon-button). Gets --lv-color-muted-bg background, sits inside the group border frame, no own border. Purely decorative addons should have aria-hidden="true" on the addon container OR on the icon partial (the icon partial already emits aria-hidden by default). An addon containing an interactive child (button) must NOT have aria-hidden on the container. INNER CONTROL BORDER STRIPPING: inner and

items.name: input-otp
items.description: A segmented one-time-code input rendered server-side: N native single-character <input> slots + a hidden `name` mirror that submits the joined value in a plain POST form (zero wire round-trips mid-entry). The auto-advance / paste-fill / backspace-nav choreography is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer. Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-input-otp> Lit island. WAI one-time-password-field ARIA (role=group + per-slot Character N of M labels).

files.content:
<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui input-otp partial -- a segmented one-time-code input rendered SERVER-SIDE (ADR-0012). N native single-character s + a hidden `name` mirror; the small auto-advance / paste / backspace-nav behaviour is a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (input-otp.enhancer.ts), NOT inline script. Pairs with registry/jte/input-otp.enhancer.ts. TIER: PARTIAL (+ enhancer). The OTP value is NOT server state mid-entry: there is nothing for the server to decide between keystrokes, so round-tripping each keystroke over the wire would be pure chat for no gain. The slots are server-rendered native inputs; the value is form-submitted (the server binds it at POST via the hidden mirror under `name`), so it is server-bound at the moment that matters. The only client bit is the focus choreography (auto-advance, paste-fill, backspace step-back), which is a tiny typed-TS module the strict CSP accepts (no inline on* handlers). STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI one-time-password-field (Radix), the model the dropped Lit island cited: a role="group" of real slots, each aria-label="Character N of M", the per-type inputmode, autocomplete="one-time-code" on the first slot so the platform SMS-autofill fills the whole code, and a visually-hidden mirror so `new FormData(form)` sees the joined value with zero JS. Native inputs beat a custom widget: the platform owns focus, IME, paste and autofill; the enhancer only nudges focus between slots. SEPARATOR + GROUPS: `groupSize` splits the slots into explicit visual groups (shadcn's InputOTPGroup), with an InputOTPSeparator (a Lucide dash) rendered between groups. `groupSize=3` on a length-6 code gives `***-***`. The separator is decorative (aria-hidden); the slots stay one continuous keyboard + value sequence (the enhancer indexes by data-otp-slot, not by group). onComplete: when set, the enhancer fires a `lievit:otp-complete` CustomEvent (detail = the joined value) on the root once every slot is filled, and -- if `onComplete="submit"` -- submits the enclosing
. Wire it to a wire action via the data-otp-complete attribute (the consuming wire template reads it), or listen for the event. Server-first: still no per-keystroke round-trip. A11y (WAI one-time-password-field): div[role=group][aria-label] wrapping N inputs each aria-label="Character N of M"; aria-invalid mirrored on the group + slots. Separators are aria-hidden decorations between visual groups. Params: name String submit field name; the joined value POSTs under it via the hidden mirror length int number of code slots (default 6) value String prefilled value (default empty); chars distributed across the slots type String "numeric" | "alphanumeric" | "alpha" (default "numeric"); drives inputmode + pattern mask boolean render slots as password fields (default false) disabled boolean disable every slot (default false) invalid boolean error ring + aria-invalid (default false) groupSize int split slots into groups of this size with a separator between (0 = no grouping) onComplete String "submit" submits the form when filled; any non-blank value enables the complete event label String accessible label for the slot group (default "One-time code") Usage: @template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", length = 6, type = "numeric") @template.lievit.input-otp(name = "otp", length = 6, groupSize = 3, onComplete = "submit") import the enhancer once from your entry module: import "./lievit/input-otp.enhancer"; --%> @param String name @param int length = 6 @param String value = "" @param String type = "numeric" @param boolean mask = false @param boolean disabled = false @param boolean invalid = false @param int groupSize = 0 @param String onComplete = null @param String label = "One-time code" !{var inputMode = type.equals("numeric") ? "numeric" : "text";} !{var pattern = type.equals("numeric") ? "[0-9]*" : (type.equals("alpha") ? "[a-zA-Z]*" : "[a-zA-Z0-9]*");} !{var slotType = mask ? "password" : "text";} !{var grouped = groupSize > 0;} !{var hasComplete = onComplete != null && !onComplete.isBlank();}
@for(int i = 0; i < length; i++) @if(grouped && i > 0 && i % groupSize == 0) @template.lievit.icon(name = "minus", size = "1rem") @endif !{var ch = i < value.length() ? String.valueOf(value.charAt(i)) : "";} !{var runStart = i == 0 || (grouped && i % groupSize == 0);} !{var runEnd = i == length - 1 || (grouped && (i + 1) % groupSize == 0);} !{var borderColor = invalid ? "var(--lv-color-danger)" : "var(--lv-color-border)";} !{var leftBorder = runStart ? ("1px solid " + borderColor) : "none";} !{var radius = runStart && runEnd ? "var(--lv-radius-sm)" : (runStart ? "var(--lv-radius-sm) 0 0 var(--lv-radius-sm)" : (runEnd ? "0 var(--lv-radius-sm) var(--lv-radius-sm) 0" : "0"));} @endfor

items.name: kbd
items.description: A keyboard-key badge (v-next): the small recessed pill that shows a shortcut key (Ctrl, Cmd, Esc) or a chord (Ctrl+K). Takes a List<String> of key names; a single-entry list renders one <kbd>; a multi-entry list renders an outer <kbd> wrapping inner <kbd> elements with a separator. Size scale (sm|md|lg); ariaLabel smart attr; monospace font via --lv-font-mono. Renders the semantic <kbd> element (screen-reader-correct), token-styled with muted fill + 1px border. Server-first PARTIAL, zero JS, CSP-clean.

files.content: <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui kbd partial (v-next) -- a keyboard-key badge: the small recessed pill that shows a shortcut key (Ctrl, Cmd, Esc) or a chord (Ctrl+K). TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure presentation, no client state. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): renders the semantic element (the screen-reader-correct element for keyboard input). Single key: one containing the key text. Chord (keys.size() > 1): outer wrapping one inner per key, separated by a Unicode separator so the chord reads as individual keys to AT. A11y: the native element is the correct semantic for keyboard input (HTML spec). pointer-events-none / select-none keep it from being selected as text, matching shadcn. ariaLabel on the outer element (smart attr: omitted when null) supports screen-reader overrides when the caller wants a bespoke announcement. TOKEN decision: bg from --lv-color-muted-bg, text from --lv-color-muted-fg (the same pair avatar/empty use), border from --lv-color-border (the 1px recessed "key" look), shadow from --lv-shadow-xs (slight depth), font from --lv-font-mono (monospace convention, confirmed present at tokens.css line 198). SIZE scale (text + padding, not height -- is inline): sm text-xs px-space-2 py-space-1 md text-sm px-space-2 py-space-1 (default) lg text-base px-space-3 py-space-1 Params: java.util.List keys REQUIRED: ordered list of key names (["Ctrl", "K"]). String size = "md" "sm" | "md" | "lg". String ariaLabel = null aria-label on the outer (omitted when null). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the outer . String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC strings only. Usage: @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Esc")) @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Ctrl", "K")) @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Cmd", "Shift", "P"), size = "sm") @@template.lievit.kbd(keys = java.util.List.of("Enter"), ariaLabel = "Premi Invio per confermare") --%> @param java.util.List keys @param String size = "md" @param String ariaLabel = null @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" !{var sizeClass = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-xs)] px-[var(--lv-space-2)] py-[var(--lv-space-1)]"; case "lg" -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-base)] px-[var(--lv-space-3)] py-[var(--lv-space-1)]"; default -> "text-[length:var(--lv-text-sm)] px-[var(--lv-space-2)] py-[var(--lv-space-1)]"; };} !{var base = "inline-flex items-center select-none rounded-[var(--lv-radius-sm)] border border-[var(--lv-color-border)] bg-[var(--lv-color-muted-bg)] text-[var(--lv-color-muted-fg)] font-[var(--lv-font-mono)] shadow-[var(--lv-shadow-xs)]";} !{var innerBase = "inline-flex items-center";} !{boolean isChord = keys.size() > 1;} @if(isChord) @for(int i = 0; i < keys.size(); i++)@if(i > 0) @endif${keys.get(i)}@endfor @else ${keys.isEmpty() ? "" : keys.get(0)} @endif

@import java.util.Map <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui key-value partial -- a read-only two-column table of key -> value pairs, the detail-page primitive Filament's KeyValueEntry needs (a map shown as a small ruled table inside an infolist). The read-only twin of a form's key-value REPEATER: here the pairs are already resolved (the kit's KeyValueEntry.resolveMap output), the template only iterates them. TIER (planning two-tier model): STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout over an already-projected ordered map; no client state, no lifecycle => not a Lit island, not a wire component. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the semantic shape for a labelled key/value grid is a real with a (the keyLabel / valueLabel headers) + a of . shadcn has no dedicated key-value export (its examples compose a bordered table); Filament's KeyValueEntry renders exactly this two-column ruled table, so we ship it as the primitive. NOT description-list (that is a dt/dd grid for a record's fields, not a homogeneous map with column headers); NOT data-table (no sorting / selection / pagination). A native
key value
carries the implicit table role (WAI-ARIA 1.2 §4.c prohibits a redundant prohibits duplicating the implicit ARIA role of a host-language semantic element). TOKEN FIDELITY: the border reads --lv-color-border, the radius --lv-radius-md, headers read --lv-color-muted-fg, cell text --lv-color-fg, padding the space scale; a retheme retints the whole table without a markup edit. bordered: wraps the table in a card chrome (border + radius + overflow:hidden) when true (default). striped: alternates a surface tint on even rows via --lv-color-surface for readability on long maps. emptyMessage: when set, renders a full-width cell when the pairs map is empty instead of an empty tbody. When null, the tbody is left empty (no row rendered, only the header). A11y: native
with labels each column via scope="col" on each
; the table is read-only content, not a control, so no extra ARIA is needed. Params: pairs Map the ordered key -> value pairs (already projected to strings). keyLabel String = "Key" the key-column header. valueLabel String = "Value" the value-column header. boolean bordered = true wrap the table in bordered card chrome (border + radius). boolean striped = false tint even rows with --lv-color-surface for readability. String emptyMessage = null message when pairs is empty; null => empty tbody. cssClass String = "" extra utility classes on the table root. Usage: @@template.lievit.key-value(pairs = pairs, keyLabel = "Field", valueLabel = "Value") @@template.lievit.key-value(pairs = pairs, striped = true, emptyMessage = "No entries yet.") --%> @param Map pairs @param String keyLabel = "Key" @param String valueLabel = "Value" @param boolean bordered = true @param boolean striped = false @param String emptyMessage = null @param String cssClass = "" !{var borderedClass = bordered ? "border border-[var(--lv-color-border)] rounded-[var(--lv-radius-md)] overflow-hidden" : "";} @if(pairs.isEmpty()) @if(emptyMessage != null) @endif @else !{int[] _idx = {0};} @for(Map.Entry pair : pairs.entrySet()) !{_idx[0]++;} !{var rowClass = (striped && _idx[0] % 2 == 0) ? "bg-[var(--lv-color-surface)]" : "";} @endfor @endif
${keyLabel} ${valueLabel}
${emptyMessage}
${pair.getKey()} ${pair.getValue()}

items.name: key-value-input
items.description: An EDITABLE repeatable key/value FORM control (Filament's KeyValue field): rows of two text inputs (key + value) with add / remove. NOT the read-only `key-value` display primitive (a static dl/dt/dd) -- this is the editable form counterpart, named key-value-INPUT to avoid the collision. Each row is two native inputs named `<name>[<i>][key]` / `<name>[<i>][value]` (the canonical form-array convention a Spring binder maps to a List/Map), so the rows POST natively with zero JS. JS-OFF an Add submit (name=`<name>__add`) and a per-row remove submit (name=`<name>__remove`, value=index) round-trip to the server, which re-renders -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer clones a hidden <template> row to add and drops a row to remove, in place, then re-indexes the survivors so the POSTed indices stay contiguous. The native inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: aria-labelled inputs (role + row number), labelled add / remove buttons, aria-live announcements. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

items.name: loading-section
items.description: Unified loading state component: three modes (spinner/skeleton/section) in one partial. spinner: SVG ring for action feedback. skeleton: shimmer layout placeholder. section: real content slot with overlay spinner for blocking async ops. role=status/region, aria-live, fullPage overlay, showTip, CSP-clean. Zero JS.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.Content @import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui loading-section partial -- unified loading state component. Three modes in one partial, selected via the `mode` param: spinner -- an animated SVG ring: use for action feedback (saving, submitting). skeleton -- a shimmer layout placeholder: use while fetching a whole content region. section -- a real content slot overlaid by a spinner when active: use for regions that show existing content but need to block interaction during an async op. TIER: PARTIAL presentational -> JTE partial (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). Pure display, no client state; the server picks the mode and the morph swaps it out once done. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): Filament v3 ships three sibling components (Loading Section, Skeleton, Spinner) with overlapping purposes and a composition hierarchy. lievit unifies them under one partial with a mode switch so the call site declares intent rather than composing primitives manually. Spinner and skeleton are also available as standalone partials (spinner.jte, skeleton.jte) for inline / narrow-context use. SELF-CONTAINED (no cross-partial deps): the spinner SVG ring and skeleton bars are inlined directly in each mode branch. This keeps loading-section.jte deployable as a single file and avoids the circular-reference risk that would arise if the section overlay called the spinner partial recursively. The standalone spinner.jte/skeleton.jte partials are equivalent; changes to the SVG ring anatomy must be mirrored in both files (intentional duplication, not drift). SPINNER SVG anatomy: background track circle (opacity 0.25) + foreground arc. Circumference = 2 * pi * 10 = 62.832; dasharray = 31.416 (half circumference); dashoffset = 10.472 (1/6 of circumference), giving a ~300-deg visible arc. viewBox="0 0 24 24" fixed; width/height attributes drive the rendered size. SKELETON shimmer: controlled by lv-skeleton--active class + skeleton.css. SECTION overlay: an aria-hidden div positioned absolute (over the content slot via relative container) showing an inlined SVG spinner ring. The content slot is the real page content; the overlay is removed when the op completes (htmx swap / morph). A11y: spinner/skeleton modes: role="status" + aria-live="polite" + aria-atomic="true" on root. section mode: root is role="region" (not a live region -- the content owns announcements); the overlay carries aria-hidden="true" so screen readers skip the decorative spinner; aria-busy="true" on the root signals the blocked state to AT. https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Roles/status_role https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/status-messages Params: String mode = "spinner" "spinner" | "skeleton" | "section". String size = "md" "sm" | "md" | "lg" -- ring diameter (spinner/overlay) or text-row height (skeleton). NOT used in section mode. String variant = "default" "default" | "primary" -- spinner stroke colour. Skeleton + section modes ignore this param. String label = "Loading..." accessible name (sr-only in spinner/skeleton; visible tip in section mode when showTip=true). boolean showTip = false section mode: render label as a visible tip text line. boolean active = true skeleton mode: shimmer animation active. int skeletonRows = 3 skeleton mode, skeletonShape=lines: number of text rows. String skeletonShape = "lines" skeleton mode: "lines"|"card"|"avatar-row"|"image"|"button-bar". boolean fullPage = false section mode: fixed viewport overlay (blocks entire page). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root element. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only. Map dataAttrs = {} SAFE dynamic data-* (value escaped via Escape.htmlAttribute). Content content = null section mode only: the real content slot. Usage: @@template.lievit.loading-section() @@template.lievit.loading-section(mode = "skeleton", skeletonShape = "avatar-row") @@template.lievit.loading-section(mode = "section", label = "Saving", showTip = true) { } @@template.lievit.loading-section(mode = "spinner", size = "lg", variant = "primary") --%> @param String mode = "spinner" @param String size = "md" @param String variant = "default" @param String label = "Loading…" @param boolean showTip = false @param boolean active = true @param int skeletonRows = 3 @param String skeletonShape = "lines" @param boolean fullPage = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param java.util.Map dataAttrs = java.util.Map.of() @param Content content = null !{var dataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{for (var e : dataAttrs.entrySet()) { if (e.getKey() != null && e.getKey().matches("[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*")) { dataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-" + e.getKey() + "=\""); Escape.htmlAttribute(e.getValue() == null ? "" : e.getValue(), dataAttrs_); dataAttrs_.writeContent("\""); } }} !{var dataAttrsMarkup = dataAttrs_.toString();} <%-- ======================================================================== SPINNER MODE ======================================================================== --%> @if("spinner".equals(mode)) !{var svgSize = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "16"; case "lg" -> "40"; default -> "24"; };} !{var strokeColor = "primary".equals(variant) ? "var(--lv-color-primary)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} ${label} <%-- ======================================================================== SKELETON MODE ======================================================================== --%> @elseif("skeleton".equals(mode)) !{var activeClass = active ? "lv-skeleton--active" : "";} !{var lineHeights = java.util.Map.of("sm", "0.75rem", "md", "1rem", "lg", "1.25rem");} !{var lineH = lineHeights.getOrDefault(size, "1rem");} <%-- Deterministic width sequence: cycles by row index mod 5 -- no DB data, no RNG. --%> !{var widths = java.util.List.of("100%", "75%", "88%", "62%", "94%");}
${label} @if("lines".equals(skeletonShape))
@for(int i = 0; i < skeletonRows; i++)
@endfor
@elseif("card".equals(skeletonShape))
@elseif("avatar-row".equals(skeletonShape)) !{var avatarSize = "sm".equals(size) ? "2rem" : "lg".equals(size) ? "3rem" : "2.5rem";}
@elseif("image".equals(skeletonShape))
@elseif("button-bar".equals(skeletonShape))
@endif
<%-- ======================================================================== SECTION MODE -- real content slot + overlay spinner ======================================================================== --%> @else !{var overlaySvgSize = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "16"; case "lg" -> "40"; default -> "24"; };} !{var overlayStrokeColor = "primary".equals(variant) ? "var(--lv-color-primary)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} !{var positionStyle = fullPage ? "position:fixed;inset:0;z-index:var(--lv-z-overlay);" : "position:relative;";}
@if(content != null) ${content} @endif <%-- Overlay: aria-hidden -- purely visual; AT sees only the aria-busy on the root. --%>
<%-- Inlined SVG ring (no cross-partial call; self-contained per inline-trivial-bits rule). --%> @if(showTip) ${label} @endif
@endif

items.description: A server-first markdown form field (Filament MarkdownEditor parity): a toolbar + a <textarea name=...> + a preview pane. The canonical control is the plain textarea, which POSTs its markdown with the surrounding <form> JS-off; a CSP-clean island adds the toolbar (wrap the current selection in markdown syntax, the pure transform is unit-tested here) and a LIVE PREVIEW that renders the markdown to SANITIZED HTML on every input. The markdown lib (e.g. marked, no eval) + sanitizer (e.g. DOMPurify) are injected by the adopter so they are pinned downstream and the preview cannot inject script under a strict CSP. Nothing about submission depends on the island.

items.docs: Render the partial with a `name` (the form field). With JS off the <textarea> is the editor and POSTs its markdown. To enhance: `npm i marked dompurify`, then call enhanceMarkdownEditors({ render }) once from main.ts after startLievit(), passing render = (md) => DOMPurify.sanitize(marked.parse(md, { async: false })). SANITIZE is mandatory: the preview sets innerHTML, so the renderer MUST return sanitized HTML (DOMPurify), else markdown raw-HTML passthrough becomes XSS. Without a render, the toolbar still works and the preview toggle is simply not wired. The toolbar commands (bold/italic/code/link/heading/quote/ul) wrap the current textarea selection in markdown syntax (the same edit a user could type); the pure applyCommand transform is unit-tested. On submit you receive the field as raw markdown; render + sanitize again server-side before display. Adjust the template name freely once copied.

items.name: modal
items.description: A centred modal dialog (shadcn Dialog), STATIC + trigger-driven, with ZERO JS. Built on the native HTML <dialog> element: the trigger is a real <button command="show-modal" commandfor> (declarative open, no script, like popovertarget) and every close path (header x, backdrop, Escape) is a native <form method="dialog"> submit / the browser's modal default. The browser renders it in the top layer, traps focus, paints the ::backdrop, and exposes it as a modal dialog to AT, so the partial is CSP-clean with no enhancer. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby). Body + footer are OWNED Content slots, never <slot>. The presentation shell the kit's modal stack renders through: it carries the io.lievit.kit.component.ModalView affordances (closeButton, closeByClickingAway, closeByEscaping, width) and io.lievit.kit.ModalConfig chrome (heading/description, width xs..7xl/full/screen mirroring the kit Width/ModalWidth tokens, start/center alignment, submit/cancel footer slot). The trigger-driven static counterpart of the server-state-held wire dialog (registry/wire/dialog/). Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens.

items.name: native-select
items.description: Token-styled wrapper around the native <select> element: PARTIAL tier, zero JS, zero enhancer, full platform a11y for free. WAI-ARIA APG Listbox (native) -- the platform supplies role=listbox, all keyboard interaction (Tab, arrows, Home/End, type-ahead), and accessible-name binding. Three option-authoring paths with explicit precedence: content slot (hand-authored <option>/<optgroup>, value!=label) > optionGroups (Map of String to String-list, rendered as native <optgroup>) > options (flat List<String>, value==label). Height-based size scale (sm/md/lg; 'default' is back-compat alias of 'md') aligns flush with button and input of the same size in a toolbar row. Invalid state: aria-invalid + destructive border + ring (color-mix in oklch). Wire binding via l:model on the native <select> (change event -> WIRE field setter); null = plain form POST, zero JS. No attrs trusted-raw channel (no per-row dynamic wire action on this partial). CSP-clean: no inline script, no on* handler.

items.name: popover
items.description: A click-triggered floating panel anchored to a trigger, with ZERO JS. The load-bearing overlay seam (ADR-0012 blueprint R4): show/hide via the native HTML `popover` attribute + `popovertarget` (top-layer, light-dismiss, ARIA wired by the browser); position via CSS Anchor Positioning (anchor-name / position-anchor / position-area + flip-block fallback), no @floating-ui/dom. Panel content is OWNED, server-rendered markup (a Content slot), never a <slot>. Optional `title` + `description` render a PopoverHeader and make it a labelled dialog (role=dialog + aria-labelledby); an `align` axis (start/center/end via data-align + position-area inline span) and a decoupled PopoverAnchor (the popover/anchor sub-partial + `anchorId`) match shadcn. Replaces the <lv-popover> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean. For server-data-driven content + server-held open-state, see the popover-wire component.

items.name: repeater
items.description: A repeatable list of nested-schema item cards (Filament's Repeater): the same set of fields repeated N times, each in a card with add / remove (reorder grip rendered, wiring is a follow-up). The item BODY is a RECURSIVE content slot the adopter authors (itemContent), so an item can hold any field -- inputs, a select, even another repeater -- the same shape as a React component taking children. The fields inside name themselves `<name>[<i>][...]` (an indexed form-array a Spring binder maps to a List of records), so the cards POST natively with zero JS. JS-OFF an Add submit (name=`<name>__add`) and a per-item remove submit (name=`<name>__remove`, value=index) round-trip to the server, which re-renders -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer clones a hidden <template> card to add and drops a card to remove, in place, then re-indexes every nested field name + label so the POSTed indices stay contiguous. The native inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: labelled fieldset/legend, each card a labelled region (Item N), labelled add / remove buttons, aria-live. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

The canonical control is a plain <textarea name=...> that POSTs its HTML with the surrounding <form> JS-off; a CSP-clean island progressively enhances it into a TipTap (ProseMirror) WYSIWYG editor (toolbar + contenteditable surface) whose serialized HTML is written back into the SAME textarea on every change, so the form keeps POSTing the field with or without JS. items.name: rich-select
items.description: A searchable Combobox (Filament Select parity, roadmap L1) whose options are filtered SERVER-SIDE: the query binds l:model.debounce, the server re-filters the catalog and re-renders the listbox (the canonical wire/htmx typeahead, no client filtering). Supports single-select (the `selected` string, set via $set) AND multiple-select with removable chips (the `selectedValues` list, toggled via $set('toggleValue',...)), preload (eager full-catalog on mount for small sets) vs lazy debounced search (large sets), inline create-option (a no-match query arms a new value), and rich option labels (avatar/icon + subtext). Single mode: selection sets the `selected` field via $set('selected', '<value>'). Multiple mode (multiple=true): each pick / chip-remove toggles membership of its value in `selectedValues` via $set('toggleValue', '<value>'); the chosen options render as removable chips and submit as repeated hidden inputs (name=...). Preload (preload=true) shows the full catalog on mount for SMALL sets; leave it false and narrow `allOptions` server-side per keystroke for LARGE sets. allowCreate=true offers a 'Create "<query>"' affordance on a no-match query (value creation only; wire createOptionUsing server-side for a full form). Rich labels: an Option carries optional avatar (image URL) / icon (Lucide name) + subtext. Call enhanceRichSelects(runtime) once from main.ts after startLievit() for APG keyboard navigation. items.name: section
items.description: A titled, optionally collapsible container that groups fields / entries under a heading (v-next). Filament Section traits as server-pure variants: collapsible/collapsed (native details/summary), compact (tighter padding), divided (ruled header/body line), aside (header in a side rail). Four surface variants (default/outlined/elevated/ghost). A heading makes it a labelled landmark (aria-labelledby). Plain HTML + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies the section partial to your JTE root at lievit/section.jte, reached as @template.lievit.section(heading="...", content=@`...`). A non-collapsible section is a plain <section> + heading; pass collapsible=true (and collapsed=true to start closed) for a native <details> disclosure. compact tightens padding (--lv-space-3 vs --lv-space-4), divided rules a border-top between header and body, aside puts the header in a side rail grid. variant (default/outlined/elevated/ghost) tints the surface. The chevron rotate-on-open is opt-in via one adopter CSS rule keyed off [data-slot="section"][open] .lv-section-chevron; without it the section still opens + closes. Static ARIA goes in attrs; per-row dynamic data-* goes in dataAttrs (escaped). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: separator
items.description: A visual and semantic dividing line between content sections. Rendered as an <hr> element (horizontal or vertical) which carries role=separator implicitly in all browsers -- zero manual role attribute needed. Three line-stroke variants (default=solid, dashed, dotted). Optional centred label text replaces the <hr> with a flex-row [line][label][line] layout (the Ant Design Divider children use case). decorative=false by default (semantic); set decorative=true to hide the line from the a11y tree via role=presentation. No JS, no enhancer, no client state. CSP-clean. Tokens: --lv-color-border (line colour), --lv-border-width (stroke), --lv-space-4 (block margin), --lv-space-2 (inline margin for vertical), --lv-space-3 (label padding + flanking gap), --lv-text-sm + --lv-color-muted + --lv-font-sans + --lv-font-normal (label typography).

items.docs: Copies the sidebar partial to your JTE root at lievit/sidebar.jte (reached as @template.lievit.sidebar(...)), its sub-partials at lievit/sidebar/{group,item,rail,inset,menu-action,group-action}.jte (@template.lievit.sidebar.group / .item / .rail / .inset / .menu-action / .group-action), and the CSP-clean enhancer to your alias root at lievit/sidebar.enhancer.ts. Compose the nav from your typed model: a sidebar.group per section, a sidebar.item per entry (key, label, href, icon, badge, active, disabled; pass a `content` sub-tree to make an item a collapsible parent; pass an `action` slot for a trailing sidebar.menu-action). Give a group an `action` slot for a sidebar.group-action by its heading. Drop a sidebar.rail inside the nav for an edge-docked collapse toggle. Pick a variant: sidebar (default), none, floating, or inset; for inset, lay the sidebar(variant="inset") beside a sidebar.inset(<main>) in a flex row. Set each item's `active` from the route being rendered so it carries aria-current=page. Import the enhancer once from your entry module and call enhanceAllSidebars() on load (and after any DOM swap); it toggles the desktop collapsed rail (persisted under storageKey via localStorage), the mobile off-canvas overlay, the rail edge, and the Cmd/Ctrl+B shortcut. The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: skeleton
items.description: A shimmer-animated layout placeholder that reserves space while real content loads. Five shape presets (lines/card/avatar-row/image/button-bar), three sizes, active/inactive toggle. role=status + aria-live for AT; shimmer suppressed under prefers-reduced-motion (via skeleton.css). Zero JS, CSP-clean.
items.docs: Copies skeleton.jte + skeleton.css to your project. The shimmer animation uses --lv-skeleton-bg and --lv-skeleton-shimmer tokens (net-new; added to lievit-tokens.css by `lievit add skeleton`). Call as @template.lievit.skeleton(skeletonShape=..., skeletonRows=..., active=...). The source is yours: edit freely.

items.name: slide-over
items.description: A modal panel that slides in from a viewport edge (right | left | top | bottom) -- the shadcn Sheet, STATIC + trigger-driven, with ZERO JS. The modal partial pinned to an edge: same native HTML <dialog> seam (trigger <button command="show-modal" commandfor>, close via native <form method="dialog"> + the browser's Escape default), so the browser owns top-layer rendering, focus-trap, ::backdrop and AT-modal exposure -- CSP-clean, no enhancer. The `side` (data-side) pins + sizes the panel to the chosen edge; the markup carries the shadcn sheet-* data-slot contract. WAI-ARIA APG Dialog (role=dialog + aria-modal + aria-labelledby/aria-describedby). Header / body / footer are OWNED Content slots, never <slot>. The static presentation shell behind the kit's edge-anchored panels: io.lievit.kit.SlideOver (right-anchored detail) and any ModalConfig.asSlideOver(). The trigger-driven static counterpart of the server-state-held wire drawer / sheet (registry/wire/drawer/, registry/wire/sheet/), named slide-over to avoid the bare drawer/sheet wire item names. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens.
items.docs: Copies the slide-over partial to your JTE root at lievit/slide-over.jte, reached as @template.lievit.slide-over(...). Pass a unique `id` (the commandfor target), the `trigger` content and the `content` panel body (server-rendered, never a <slot>); choose the `side` ("right" | "left" | "top" | "bottom", reflected on data-side), add an optional `heading` + `description` (a labelled dialog) and a `footer` slot. Tune `closeButton`, `closeByClickingAway`. The browser owns open/close (native <dialog> showModal + Escape + focus-trap + return-focus): no JS, no enhancer. The source is yours: edit freely. For a panel whose open-state lives server-side (the SlideOver "view in panel" outcome the server drives), use the wire drawer / sheet component instead.

items.name: slider
items.description: APG Slider on a platform native input[type=range]. Single-thumb default, dual-thumb range mode, horizontal or vertical. Marks, value tooltip, disabled, aria-invalid. The slider.enhancer.ts handles --slider-pct sync, range clamp + ARIA constraint updates, and tooltip text on drag.

files.content:
@import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui slider partial -- APG Slider on a platform native input[type=range]. Single-thumb default; dual-thumb range mode (rangeMode=true); horizontal or vertical. Marks, value tooltip, disabled, aria-invalid. The enhancer handles the cases the native element does not cover: range clamp, --slider-pct sync, tooltip text update. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (slider.enhancer.ts). The native range input carries role=slider, aria-valuemin/max/now, and the full APG keyboard map (Arrow/Home/End/PageUp-Down) for free; no JS keyboard handler is needed. The enhancer's job is purely the irreducible client bits: real-time --slider-pct updates on drag/key, range-mode clamp + ARIA constraint sync, tooltip visibility toggling. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): APG Slider (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/ patterns/slider/) for single-thumb; APG Slider (Multi-Thumb) (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ ARIA/apg/patterns/slider-multithumb/) for range mode. Both URLs verified 2026-06-24. The native input[type=range] is the ACCESSIBLE SPINE -- do not replace it with a div[role=slider]. The styled overlay (track rail + fill + thumb overlay + tooltip + marks) is aria-hidden="true" throughout. The initial fill percentage (--slider-pct) is computed server-side and set as an inline CSS custom property on the root; the enhancer keeps it in sync on every interaction without a round-trip. Inventory reference: Ant Design Slider (range mode, marks, tooltip, vertical, disabled) -- no code copied, original generation. A11y (WAI-ARIA APG Slider + APG Slider Multi-Thumb): Roles: role="slider" is implicit on every native input[type=range]. ARIA -- single-thumb: aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax/aria-valuenow: native (from min/max/value attributes). aria-label: set from ariaLabel param when no visible label exists. aria-valuetext: set from ariaValuetext param for human-readable override. aria-orientation="vertical": emitted when orientation="vertical" (horizontal is default). ARIA -- range mode (two thumbs, APG multithumb rule): lower thumb: aria-valuemin="${min}" aria-valuemax="${valueHigh}" (upper value is its cap). upper thumb: aria-valuemin="${valueLow}" aria-valuemax="${max}" (lower value is its floor). Each thumb carries aria-label from ariaLabelLow/ariaLabelHigh. aria-valuetext per thumb from ariaValuetextLow/ariaValuetextHigh. Keyboard: Arrow/Home/End/PageUp-Down supplied by the platform for all thumbs. Enhancer only adds: range clamp on input event + aria-valuemin/max attribute sync. Focus: platform Tab focus on each native input. No trap. No roving tabindex. Tab order low-thumb -> high-thumb is constant; enhancer never re-sorts DOM. Styled overlay: aria-hidden="true" on every visual-only element (thumb div, track fill, tooltip, mark row). The overlay carries no role and no interactive ARIA. Disabled: native `disabled` on input[type=range]; overlay gets pointer-events-none. aria-invalid: the field partial sets it on a parent; CSS [aria-invalid] selectors on the track fill + thumb overlay recolour to --lv-color-destructive. The slider partial itself does NOT emit aria-invalid (it is inherited from the field wrapper). Params: String name form field name (plain form POST, not l:model). double value = 0 current value (single-thumb). double min = 0 minimum (aria-valuemin). double max = 100 maximum (aria-valuemax). double step = 1 keyboard/snap increment; 0 = continuous. boolean rangeMode = false dual-thumb range selection. double valueLow = 0 range-mode: lower thumb value. double valueHigh = 100 range-mode: upper thumb value. String orientation = "horizontal" "horizontal" | "vertical". boolean disabled = false dims + blocks activation; native disabled. java.util.List> marks = null tick marks: each map has "value" (numeric string) and "label" (may be blank for unlabelled tick). String showTooltip = "hover" "always" | "hover" | "never". String tooltipFormatter = null server-formatted label for current value; null = raw. String tooltipFormatterLow = null range-mode: label for valueLow. String tooltipFormatterHigh = null range-mode: label for valueHigh. String ariaLabel = null aria-label for single thumb. String ariaLabelLow = null range-mode: aria-label for lower thumb. String ariaLabelHigh = null range-mode: aria-label for upper thumb. String ariaValuetext = null aria-valuetext for single thumb. String ariaValuetextLow = null range-mode: aria-valuetext for lower thumb. String ariaValuetextHigh = null range-mode: aria-valuetext for upper thumb. String size = "md" "sm" | "md" | "lg". String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root wrapper. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) STATIC strings only (wire directives, data-testid static). NEVER feed per-row DB-derived values here. java.util.Map dataAttrs = {} SAFE escaped dynamic data-* on root (each value through Escape.htmlAttribute). Usage: @@template.lievit.slider(name = "prezzo", min = 0, max = 1000, step = 50, value = 250, ariaLabel = "Prezzo") @@template.lievit.slider(name = "fascia", rangeMode = true, min = 0, max = 1000, valueLow = 200, valueHigh = 800, ariaLabelLow = "Minimo", ariaLabelHigh = "Massimo") @@template.lievit.slider(name = "volume", orientation = "vertical", value = 60, ariaLabel = "Volume", showTooltip = "always") --%> @param String name = null @param double value = 0 @param double min = 0 @param double max = 100 @param double step = 1 @param boolean rangeMode = false @param double valueLow = 0 @param double valueHigh = 100 @param String orientation = "horizontal" @param boolean disabled = false @param java.util.List> marks = null @param String showTooltip = "hover" @param String tooltipFormatter = null @param String tooltipFormatterLow = null @param String tooltipFormatterHigh = null @param String ariaLabel = null @param String ariaLabelLow = null @param String ariaLabelHigh = null @param String ariaValuetext = null @param String ariaValuetextLow = null @param String ariaValuetextHigh = null @param String size = "md" @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" @param java.util.Map dataAttrs = java.util.Map.of() <%-- Derived booleans + orientation flag. --%> !{var isVertical = "vertical".equals(orientation);} !{var hasTicks = marks != null && !marks.isEmpty();} <%-- Fill percentage for the server-rendered initial state. Single-thumb: ((value - min) / (max - min)) * 100. Range mode: fill from valueLow to valueHigh. Guard against division by zero when min == max. --%> !{var range_ = (max - min) != 0 ? (max - min) : 1.0;} !{var pct = (int) Math.round(((value - min) / range_) * 100);} !{var pctLow = (int) Math.round(((valueLow - min) / range_) * 100);} !{var pctHigh = (int) Math.round(((valueHigh - min) / range_) * 100);} <%-- Tooltip visibility class: always=opacity-100, hover=opacity-0 (enhancer shows on hover), never=opacity-0 sr-only (hidden from layout; still in DOM for a11y backup). --%> !{var tooltipVisClass = "always".equals(showTooltip) ? "opacity-100" : "opacity-0";} !{var tooltipSrClass = "never".equals(showTooltip) ? " pointer-events-none select-none" : "";} <%-- Tooltip label displayed at server render time. --%> !{var tooltipLabel = tooltipFormatter != null ? tooltipFormatter : String.valueOf((int) value);} !{var tooltipLabelLow = tooltipFormatterLow != null ? tooltipFormatterLow : String.valueOf((int) valueLow);} !{var tooltipLabelHigh = tooltipFormatterHigh != null ? tooltipFormatterHigh : String.valueOf((int) valueHigh);} <%-- Size-specific token classes for thumb diameter and track thickness. All class strings are COMPLETE + static so the Tailwind scanner picks them up. --%> !{var thumbSize = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "var(--lv-space-3-5)"; case "lg" -> "var(--lv-space-5-5)"; default -> "var(--lv-space-4-5)"; };} !{var trackThickness = switch (size) { case "sm" -> "var(--lv-space-1)"; case "lg" -> "var(--lv-space-2)"; default -> "var(--lv-space-1-5)"; };} <%-- Root layout: horizontal = flex-row, vertical = flex-col. --%> !{var rootLayout = isVertical ? "flex flex-col items-center" : "flex flex-row items-center";} <%-- SAFE dynamic data-* fragment: build once, emit with $unsafe. JTE forbids @for in attribute-NAME position; this sidesteps it correctly. --%> !{var dataAttrs_ = new StringOutput();} !{for (var e : dataAttrs.entrySet()) { if (e.getKey() != null && e.getKey().matches("[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9-]*")) { dataAttrs_.writeContent(" data-" + e.getKey() + "=\""); Escape.htmlAttribute(e.getValue() == null ? "" : e.getValue(), dataAttrs_); dataAttrs_.writeContent("\""); } }} !{var dataAttrsMarkup = dataAttrs_.toString();} <%-- Root wrapper: data-slot, data-size, data-orientation, data-range, --slider-pct. data-lievit-enhancer="slider" triggers the slider.enhancer.ts lifecycle hook. --slider-pct drives the fill width/height and the thumb translate via CSS. data-tooltip-formatter / data-tooltip-formatter-low / data-tooltip-formatter-high carry the formatter template to the enhancer (raw strings -- author-controlled, never DB-derived without explicit sanitization by the calling template). --%>
<%-- TRACK LAYER (aria-hidden; purely visual rail + fill) --%>
<%-- Track fill: coloured region between min and thumb (single) or between the two thumbs (range). Width/height driven by CSS --slider-pct; updated by the enhancer on drag. --%>
<%-- NATIVE INPUT LAYER: the real accessible spine. Single-thumb: one input. Range mode: two inputs (low then high, constant DOM order per APG multithumb rule -- the tab order must not change when thumbs cross). --%> @if(rangeMode) <%-- LOW THUMB --%> <%-- HIGH THUMB --%> @else @endif <%-- STYLED THUMB OVERLAY (aria-hidden; visual only; the real interaction is on the native input). Single thumb: position driven by --slider-pct. Range mode: low thumb at --slider-pct, high thumb at --slider-pct-high. The overlay is pointer-events-none so clicks pass through to the native input above. --%> @if(rangeMode) <%-- Low thumb overlay --%>
<%-- High thumb overlay --%>
<%-- Tooltips: range mode (low + high). Always in DOM; CSS visibility controlled by showTooltip. aria-hidden: the a11y value is on the native input via aria-valuenow/aria-valuetext. --%>
${tooltipLabelLow}
${tooltipLabelHigh}
@else <%-- Single thumb overlay --%>
<%-- Single tooltip. Always in DOM regardless of showTooltip; CSS opacity controls visibility. --%>
${tooltipLabel}
@endif <%-- MARKS ROW (aria-hidden; visual decoration only; a11y value travels via aria-valuetext). --%> @if(hasTicks)
@for(java.util.Map mark : marks) !{var markVal = mark.get("value");} !{var markLabel = mark.get("label");} !{var markPct = markVal != null ? (int) Math.round(((Double.parseDouble(markVal) - min) / range_) * 100) : 0;}
<%-- Tick dot --%> <%-- Tick label: only when non-blank --%> @if(markLabel != null && !markLabel.isBlank()) ${markLabel} @endif
@endfor
@endif

items.name: spinner
items.description: An animated SVG ring (background circle + foreground arc) that signals an indeterminate background operation. Two variants (default/primary), three sizes (sm/md/lg), role=status + aria-live for AT, animate-spin + motion-reduce:animate-none for reduced-motion. Zero JS, CSP-clean, inline-context safe (<span> root).

files.content:
@import gg.jte.output.StringOutput @import gg.jte.html.escape.Escape <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui spinner partial -- an animated SVG ring that signals an indeterminate background operation in progress. TIER: PARTIAL presentational -> JTE partial (ADR-0012 server-first pivot). Pure display, no client state: the server renders it or not; the morph removes it when done. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): an SVG ring (background circle + foreground arc, both stroke=currentColor). The arc uses stroke-dasharray to produce a 300-degree chasing arc; the SVG rotates via Tailwind animate-spin (a standard rotate keyframe). Ant Design Spin and react-aria both use a rotating SVG ring for their spinner; lievit generates an original variant over --lv-* tokens. prefers-reduced-motion: animate-spin suppressed via motion-reduce:animate-none (Tailwind built-in). A11y (WAI-ARIA role=status): role="status" + aria-live="polite" + aria-atomic="true" + aria-label on the root (explicit mirrors of the implicit defaults for cross-AT consistency, per MDN recommendation). A sr-only span carries the label text inside the live region so aria-atomic="true" announces the full text. The decorative SVG is aria-hidden="true". No focus management -- a loading indicator must NOT receive focus on update (WCAG 4.1.3). https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/Accessibility/ARIA/Roles/status_role https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/status-messages SVG ring anatomy: background track circle (opacity 0.25) + foreground arc. Circumference = 2 * pi * 10 = 62.832; dasharray = 31.416 (half circumference = 180-deg arc); dashoffset = 10.472 (1/6 of circumference) shifts the arc start, giving a 300-deg visible arc. The SVG is sized via width/height attributes (sm=16px, md=24px, lg=40px) over a fixed viewBox="0 0 24 24" so the proportions are identical at every scale. VARIANT colours: default uses --lv-color-muted-fg (neutral busy); primary uses --lv-color-primary (branded busy, e.g. inside a primary button). Both derive from the --lv-* token set so they retheme automatically. Inline context: the root is a (not
) so the spinner can be composed inside inline or flex contexts (e.g. inside a switch thumb span or a toast flex row) without generating an invalid block-inside-inline nesting. role=status on a is valid per ARIA 1.2 (roles are orthogonal to HTML element semantics). Params: String size = "md" "sm" (16px) | "md" (24px, default) | "lg" (40px) -- ring diameter. String variant = "default" "default" (--lv-color-muted-fg) | "primary" (--lv-color-primary). String label = "Loading..." accessible name; visually hidden (sr-only). Pass "" when the spinner is inside an aria-hidden parent (decorative context). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the root. String attrs = "" TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only. items.name: switch
items.description: A binary toggle control following the WAI-ARIA APG Switch pattern. Primary variant: <button role="switch" aria-checked> — the native button gives role + Enter/Space + focus for free, no JS. Form variant (asCheckbox=true): <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> for submitting with a form. The thumb slide is driven by CSS attribute selectors (zero JS, CSP-clean). Height-based sm/md/lg sizes (18/22/28px track, --lv-space-5/6/7 thumb) align flush with form controls in a toolbar row. Supports loading state (spinner in thumb, aria-busy), inner on/off labels (aria-hidden, Ant Design style), leading/trailing Content slots for label composition, and the full SAFE wire escaping channel (wireClick + wireArgs + dataAttrs) matching button.jte. The toggle action is wired by the consuming WIRE template; the partial renders the styled snapshot. Net-new tokens: --lv-space-7, --lv-opacity-disabled, --lv-motion-switch-thumb (with prefers-reduced-motion suppression).

The partial renders a <button role="switch" aria-checked> (primary) or <input type="checkbox" role="switch"> (asCheckbox=true) with a sliding thumb. Size is height-based: sm (18px track), md (22px, default), lg (28px). Wire a toggle SAFELY with wireClick="toggle" plus wireArgs=Map.of("id", row.id()) — same escaping channel as button.jte, no hand-rolled $unsafe needed. Pass leading/trailing (gg.jte.Content) to compose a visible label. Pass ariaLabel or ariaLabelledBy — a switch without an accessible name is an a11y violation. items.name: tabs
items.description: A tab bar + panels where the active tab is server state; only the active panel is shown. Server-first WIRE component (ADR-0012): the active id lives in typed Java, the server re-renders, the client morphs. Replaces the <lv-tabs> Lit island. WAI-ARIA APG tabs (tablist > tab[aria-selected][aria-controls] + tabpanel[aria-labelledby], roving tabindex). An HTMX content-swap recipe is the lighter alternative for independent panel fragments (see docs).
items.docs: Register TabsComponent as a PROTOTYPE-scoped @Bean (a fresh instance per stateless wire call). Seed `tabIds` + `labels` (index-aligned), optional `disabledIds`, as locked server config, then render it via the wire mount / `@template.lievit.tabs(...)`. Each tab switches with `l:click="$set('active','<id>')"` (the active tab is a scalar, so the $set magic IS the select; no custom action needed); the @LievitRender hook falls back to the first enabled tab and refuses a disabled/unknown active id. The panel BODY per tab is OWNED markup inside the `data-tabs-panel` div: edit it in this copied-in template (there is no <slot>). HTMX alternative: drop the wire and give each tab link an `hx-get` to a controller endpoint returning the panel partial, swapped into the panel container, when the panels are independent server fragments. The `@LievitComponent(template="lievit/tabs")` name assumes the .jte landed at your JTE root under lievit/; adjust the package + template name freely once copied (the source is yours).

items.name: tags-input
items.description: A free-text TAGS field (Filament TagsInput; shadcn composes Badge + Input): committed tags shown as removable chips + a text input to add more, with optional suggestions. The committed tags POST as a REPEATED `name` via one hidden <input> per chip, so the set submits with zero JS. JS-OFF an explicit Add submit button (name=`<name>__add`) and a per-chip remove submit (name=`<name>__remove`) round-trip to the server, which appends / drops the tag and re-renders -- a working no-JS path. JS-ON a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer makes the editing in-place: Enter / comma / Add appends a chip + hidden input without a round-trip, the chip x deletes it, Backspace-on-empty pops the last, and suggestions are a quick-pick; an aria-live region announces changes. The hidden inputs stay the form source of truth. WAI-ARIA: labelled add-input, aria-labelled remove buttons, live announcements. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, CSP-clean.

items.name: theme-switcher
items.description: A three-state (light / dark / system) theme-preference control. Three variants: icon (compact toolbar of three icon-only toggle buttons), labeled (icon + text per option), icon-labeled (single cycling button). Persists the user's choice to localStorage and reflects it as data-theme on the configured root element (the single repoint mechanism the lievit token system uses). Server-rendered as a static HTML frame (no wire round-trip — the preference is a client-side fact); the colocated typed-TS enhancer reads localStorage on mount, syncs aria-pressed states, manages roving tabindex in the toolbar, applies data-theme to the root, and tracks prefers-color-scheme live when system is chosen. APG Toolbar + APG Toggle Button (aria-pressed) pattern. CSP-clean: zero inline script, zero on* handlers.

TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (theme-switcher.enhancer.ts, colocated). The control structure + initial state are server-rendered (CSP-clean, zero inline script). The irreducibly-client behaviour -- reading prefers-color-scheme, writing localStorage, toggling data-theme on the root, keeping aria-pressed in sync -- lives entirely in the colocated typed-TS enhancer that binds off data-lievit-enhancer="theme-switcher". items.docs: Copies the theme-switcher partial to lievit/theme-switcher.jte and the colocated typed-TS enhancer to lievit/theme-switcher.enhancer.ts. Import the enhancer in your entry module and register it with the lievit runtime: registerEnhancer("theme-switcher", ThemeSwitcherEnhancer). The partial renders a display:none root (to avoid flash-of-wrong-state); the enhancer removes the guard synchronously on mount after syncing aria-pressed. Three variants via the `variant` param: icon (default, compact toolbar), labeled (icon + text), icon-labeled (single cycling button). Size via `size`: sm/md (default)/lg — height-based, toolbar-aligned. `storageKey` names the localStorage key (default lievit-theme); `rootSelector` names the element that gets data-theme (default html); `defaultTheme` is the fallback (default system). `showSystemOption=false` reduces to a two-state light/dark toggle. Labels are i18n-friendly: set labelLight/labelDark/labelSystem from the Spring controller's i18n layer. The enhancer applies data-theme, never toggles a class (ADR-0005 single repoint mechanism). Multiple instances on the same page are allowed; they stay in sync via the shared localStorage key.

items.name: toast
items.description: A transient overlay notification rendered server-side from a flash / notification: severity drives both colour and the ARIA live role (danger/warning are assertive role=alert, the rest are polite role=status) plus a leading Lucide icon. Variants cover the sonner surface: severity (info/success/warning/danger), a neutral `default`, a `loading` spinner, and `promise` (the resolved/rejected outcome rendered server-first). A single toast fixes itself to one of the six positions (top/bottom x left/center/right); render several inside the toast/viewport stack container (data-slot=toast-viewport). The auto-dismiss timer + dismiss button are a CSP-clean typed-TS enhancer (no wire round-trip: a shown toast needs no server decision). Server-first PARTIAL + enhancer (ADR-0012); replaces the <lv-toast> Lit island.

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui toast/viewport partial -- the fixed stack container that holds several toasts at one corner (sonner's Toaster region; data-slot="toast-viewport"). Server-rendered, zero JS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Pure layout (a fixed-corner flex column with a gap), no client state: the server already knows which toasts to show, so it renders them stacked. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): sonner renders ONE fixed region per corner that column- stacks its toasts. We reproduce that with a fixed flex column anchored to the chosen `position` (the six sonner corners) and a token gap between toasts. Bottom positions stack column-reverse so a new toast appears nearest the edge and pushes the older ones away from it, matching sonner. The toasts inside are rendered with standalone=false so they flow in this column instead of fixing themselves. A11y: a plain layout container; each child toast owns its own role=alert/status live region, so the viewport itself carries no role. items.name: tooltip
items.description: A hover/focus-triggered floating description panel. WAI-ARIA APG Tooltip pattern: bubble carries role="tooltip" + a stable id; tooltip.enhancer.ts wires aria-describedby on the first focusable descendant at mount (or the wrapper span when no focusable descendant exists) and removes it on destroy. Show/hide timing: hover delay (configurable, default 600ms) + immediate focus, Escape-while-visible dismiss (document-level capture listener, registered only while open), hover persistence (pointer entering the bubble cancels the hide timer). Native popover="manual" + CSS Anchor Positioning for positioning -- the shared popover seam. No round-trip: open state is ephemeral, fires no wire actions. Four variants (default/info/warning/destructive) map to the shared intent token pairs. CSS-only arrow (lv-tooltip__arrow pseudo-element). CSP-clean, no inline handlers, no eval.
