@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui tooltip partial -- a hover/focus-triggered floating description panel. TIER: PARTIAL + ENH (tooltip.enhancer.ts, minimal). The content is static server-rendered text; the open state is a purely client-side ephemeral concern (mouse position / keyboard focus is not worth a round-trip). The irreducible client piece -- show/hide timing (hover delay + instant focus, dismiss on blur/mouseleave/Esc), aria-describedby binding, and native popover attribute management -- lives in tooltip.enhancer.ts. Server renders both the trigger wrapper and the bubble (hidden via popover="manual"); the enhancer surfaces them on demand. STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): WAI-ARIA APG Tooltip pattern (https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/tooltip/) + tooltip ARIA role (https://w3c.github.io/aria/#tooltip). The bubble carries role="tooltip" and a stable id. The enhancer wires aria-describedby on the first focusable descendant of the wrapper (or the wrapper itself) at mount -- NOT server-side, because the trigger element identity is a rendering detail, not a template concern. Native popover="manual" is used so the UA hides the bubble by default and the enhancer calls showPopover()/hidePopover() explicitly. The popover="auto" mode is intentionally avoided: it adds UA light-dismiss-on-click, which conflicts with the hover/focus-driven show model. CSS Anchor Positioning (position-anchor + position-area) is the shared popover seam for all floating surfaces in lievit; the tooltip does not hand-roll getBoundingClientRect() or position:absolute math. A11y (WAI-ARIA Tooltip): - role="tooltip" on the bubble (server-rendered, static). - id on the bubble matches data-lievit-tooltip-id on the wrapper; enhancer resolves the pairing and sets aria-describedby at onInit; removes at onDestroy. - Keyboard: Tab into trigger -> shows immediately (focusin ignores delay). Tab/Shift+Tab out -> hides immediately (no hide-delay on focusout). Escape while visible -> dismisses; focus stays on trigger (APG rule: tooltip is non-modal, no focus trap). The Esc handler is registered on document (capture) only while the bubble is open and removed on hide. - Hover persistence: pointer from trigger to bubble cancels the hide timer (APG rule). The enhancer hooks pointerenter on the bubble to cancel the pending hide. - The tooltip never receives keyboard focus (APG rule); no roving tabindex. - No live region: content surfaces via aria-describedby, not aria-live. - Bubble omitted when content is blank (role="tooltip" on an empty element is a violation). Params: String id REQUIRED. Unique id stamped on the bubble and mirrored into data-lievit-tooltip-id on the wrapper; the enhancer reads these to wire aria-describedby. String content REQUIRED. Tooltip text (plain text; HTML-escaped by JTE's default ${} -- never $unsafe; never the sole carrier of essential information). gg.jte.Content trigger REQUIRED. The element the tooltip describes (button, icon, link, text span); rendered inside the wrapper span. String variant "default" | "info" | "warning" | "destructive". Intent maps to the shared token pairs (--lv-color- bg + -fg text). "default" uses --lv-color-popover so all floating surfaces share a look at rest. String placement "top" | "top-start" | "top-end" | "bottom" | "bottom-start" | "bottom-end" | "left" | "right" -- CSS Anchor Positioning preferred placement (position-area). Falls back gracefully via position-try-fallbacks. Default "top". int delay Hover show-delay in ms (default 600). 0 = immediate (useful for icon-only buttons where the label IS the tooltip). focusin always shows immediately regardless. int hideDelay Hover hide-delay in ms after mouseleave (default 0). Non-zero lets the pointer traverse into the bubble (APG hover-persistence rule). boolean arrow Render the directional CSS-only arrow (default true). The arrow is a pseudo-element; no new token needed. String maxWidth Inline override for --_lv-tooltip-max-width (default "14rem"). A structural dimension, not a colour; safe in the style attr. boolean disabled When true the enhancer no-ops all events; bubble stays hidden. Stamped as data-lievit-tooltip-disabled on the wrapper. String cssClass Extra utility classes on the bubble. String attrs TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) -- STATIC author-typed strings only, e.g. data-testid="save-tooltip". Never interpolate DB-derived values. Usage: @@template.lievit.tooltip(id = "save-tip", content = "Save document", trigger = @@` `) @@template.lievit.tooltip(id = "del-tip", content = "Delete forever", variant = "destructive", placement = "bottom", delay = 0, trigger = @@` @@template.lievit.button(variant = "destructive", iconOnly = true, ariaLabel = "Delete", content = @@`@@template.lievit.icon(name = "trash-2", size = "1rem")`) `) --%> @param String id @param String content @param gg.jte.Content trigger @param String variant = "default" @param String placement = "top" @param int delay = 600 @param int hideDelay = 0 @param boolean arrow = true @param String maxWidth = "14rem" @param boolean disabled = false @param String cssClass = "" @param String attrs = "" <%-- CSS Anchor Positioning: map the placement param to a CSS position-area value. The spec placement vocabulary mirrors floating-ui/Radix and maps to the two-keyword position-area syntax (block-position inline-position). --%> !{var positionArea = switch (placement) { case "top-start" -> "top span-right"; case "top-end" -> "top span-left"; case "bottom" -> "bottom center"; case "bottom-start" -> "bottom span-right"; case "bottom-end" -> "bottom span-left"; case "left" -> "left center"; case "right" -> "right center"; default -> "top center"; // "top" (the default) };} <%-- Variant -> bg and fg token resolution (the shared intent vocabulary). "default" maps to the popover surface so all floating surfaces share a look at rest. No new token needed: the existing --lv-color-popover family is the correct bucket. --%> !{var variantBg = switch (variant) { case "info" -> "var(--lv-color-info)"; case "warning" -> "var(--lv-color-warning)"; case "destructive" -> "var(--lv-color-destructive)"; default -> "var(--lv-color-popover)"; };} !{var variantFg = switch (variant) { case "info" -> "var(--lv-color-info-fg)"; case "warning" -> "var(--lv-color-warning-fg)"; case "destructive" -> "var(--lv-color-destructive-fg)"; default -> "var(--lv-color-popover-fg)"; };} <%-- The anchor name is set inline on the wrapper span via style so CSS Anchor Positioning can resolve the bubble's position-anchor reference. Both the anchor-name and position-anchor use the same CSS custom ident derived from the id param. --%> !{var anchorName = "--" + id + "-anchor";} <%-- trigger slot: the element whose focus/hover shows the tooltip --%> ${trigger} <%-- tooltip bubble: always present in the DOM, hidden via popover="manual" until the enhancer calls showPopover(). Omitted entirely when content is blank so a role="tooltip" is never present on an empty or missing element (APG + ARIA spec). --%> @if(content != null && !content.isBlank())
${content}@if(arrow)@endif
@endif

items.docs: Copies the tooltip partial to your JTE root at lievit/tooltip.jte. Requires tooltip.enhancer.ts registered in your runtime (installTooltip(runtime)). Call as @template.lievit.tooltip(id="save-tip", content="Save document", trigger=@`<button>Save</button>`). id is REQUIRED and must be unique per page (it becomes the bubble's id and the aria-describedby value). The enhancer wires aria-describedby at mount -- do NOT set it in the trigger slot markup. variant selects the intent tone (default/info/warning/destructive). placement (default "top") sets the CSS Anchor Positioning preferred side; falls back via position-try-fallbacks. delay (default 600ms) is the hover show-delay; focus always shows immediately. Set delay=0 for icon-only buttons where the tooltip IS the label. hideDelay (default 0) allows the pointer to traverse into the bubble. Set arrow=false to suppress the directional pointer. disabled=true makes the enhancer no-op all events. attrs is TRUSTED raw ($unsafe) for STATIC author-typed attribute strings only -- never interpolate DB-derived values. The --lv-z-tooltip: 950 token is NET-NEW; add it to your lievit-tokens.css :root and .dark blocks.

items.name: wizard
items.description: A multi-step form (the Filament Wizard / Wizard\Step, the io.lievit.kit.schema.Wizard + io.lievit.kit.WizardAction view-models): a numbered step header where each step carries data-state complete/current/upcoming (a completed-check glyph when done, a number otherwise) with separators between steps, over the current step's content, and a footer of prev / cancel / next / submit actions. The cursor is server-owned; advancing is a real wire-action POST the adopter wires (next/previous/submit), never inline script. Reuses icon + separator. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.

@import gg.jte.Content <%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui wizard partial -- a multi-step form (the Filament Wizard / Wizard\Step, the io.lievit.kit.schema.Wizard + io.lievit.kit.WizardAction view-models): a numbered STEP HEADER (each step a wizard/step item with a number / completed-check, label, and a separator between steps) over the current step's CONTENT, with a FOOTER of prev / cancel / next / submit actions. Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012), ZERO shipped JS. TIER DECISION (why PARTIAL, not WIRE): the wizard's CURSOR (which step is current, which are complete) is server-owned data the Wizard driver already tracks (currentStep()); the partial renders the header + the current step's body + the footer for THAT cursor. Advancing (next / previous / submit) is a real wire-action POST the adopter wires on the footer buttons (an l:click or a form submit) -- the kit's WizardActionComponent drives next()/previous() and calls submit() on the last step. This partial is the pure VIEW of one cursor position; the host owns the state machine. (The kit's wizard-action.jte WIRE template composes THIS partial's chrome with l:click hooks; the registry ships the presentational primitive.) STRUCTURE (scientific decision rule): the Filament wizard header is a numbered stepper -- Radix/shadcn has no stepper, so this mirrors Filament's bespoke chrome with the shadcn data-state idiom. Each step carries data-state="complete" | "current" | "upcoming"; a complete step shows a check glyph instead of its number, a current step is filled, an upcoming step is muted. Steps are an
    (ordered, the natural numbering); a separator (the lievit-ui separator) sits between consecutive steps. The body is the `content` slot (the current step's fields); the footer is the `footer` slot (wizard/footer with the nav buttons). The currentStep index drives which step is `current` and which earlier steps are `complete`. A11y (WAI-ARIA): the header is an
      labelled "Progress"; the current step carries aria-current="step" so assistive tech announces the position; each step states its number + label as text (never the visual number alone); a complete step's check is aria-hidden (the "complete" state is conveyed by aria-label on the step). The body region is labelled by the current step's label. Params: String id = "lv-wizard" the wizard root id (labels the body region). Unique/page. int currentStep = 0 the 0-based current step index (drives complete/current). gg.jte.Content steps the step header items (wizard/step list, in order). gg.jte.Content content the current step's body (fields). gg.jte.Content footer = null the nav footer (wizard/footer: prev / cancel / next / submit). String label = "Progress" the step header's accessible name. String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the wizard root. Usage: @@template.lievit.wizard( currentStep = wizard.currentStep(), steps = @@` @@for(int i = 0; i < wizard.steps().size(); i++) @@template.lievit.wizard.step( index = i, label = wizard.steps().get(i).label(), state = i < wizard.currentStep() ? "complete" : (i == wizard.currentStep() ? "current" : "upcoming"), last = i == wizard.steps().size() - 1) @@endfor `, content = @@``, footer = @@`@@template.lievit.wizard.footer( showPrevious = wizard.currentStep() > 0, showSubmit = wizard.currentStep() == wizard.stepCount() - 1)`) --%> @param String id = "lv-wizard" @param int currentStep = 0 @param gg.jte.Content steps @param gg.jte.Content content @param gg.jte.Content footer = null @param String label = "Progress" @param String cssClass = "" !{var bodyLabelId = id + "-current-label";}
        ${steps}
      ${content}
      @if(footer != null)
      ${footer}
      @endif

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui wizard/step partial -- one item of the wizard step header (the Filament Wizard\Step in the progress strip, an io.lievit.kit.schema.Wizard.Step): a numbered marker (or a completed-check when done), the step label + optional description, and a separator trailing it (except after the last step). Server-first, no JS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. The state (complete / current / upcoming) is server-owned data the Wizard driver computes from currentStep(); the server renders the marker keyed on it. When the step is already passed and the wizard allows jumping back (goToStep), the caller can pass a `href` so the step marker is a real the browser navigates -- otherwise an inert
  • . STRUCTURE (data-state, shadcn idiom): the step carries data-state="complete" | "current" | "upcoming". A COMPLETE step's marker shows a "check" glyph (the completed-check) on a primary fill; a CURRENT step's marker shows its 1-based number on a primary fill + a ring; an UPCOMING step's marker shows its number muted with a border. The label tints with the state (fg for current/complete, muted for upcoming). The trailing separator (the lievit-ui separator) is a flex-growing hairline between this step and the next; the LAST step (`last=true`) renders no separator. A11y (WAI-ARIA): the step is an
  • stating "Step N: (jump back). String cssClass = "" extra utility classes on the step. Usage: @@template.lievit.wizard.step(index = 0, label = "Details", state = "complete") @@template.lievit.wizard.step(index = 1, label = "Review", state = "current", last = true) --%> @param int index = 0 @param String label @param String description = "" @param String state = "upcoming" @param boolean last = false @param String href = "" @param String cssClass = "" !{var complete = "complete".equals(state);} !{var current = "current".equals(state);} !{var number = index + 1;} !{var stateWord = complete ? "completed" : (current ? "current" : "upcoming");} !{var markerBg = (complete || current) ? "var(--lv-color-primary)" : "transparent";} !{var markerFg = (complete || current) ? "var(--lv-color-primary-fg)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} !{var markerBorder = (complete || current) ? "transparent" : "var(--lv-color-border)";} !{var markerRing = current ? "box-shadow: var(--lv-ring);" : "";} !{var labelColor = (complete || current) ? "var(--lv-color-fg)" : "var(--lv-color-muted-fg)";} !{var markerStyle = "width: 1.75rem; height: 1.75rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: " + markerBg + "; color: " + markerFg + "; border: 1px solid " + markerBorder + "; " + markerRing;} !{var isLink = !href.isEmpty();}
  • <%-- One tag (never a conditional / split: JTE's static checker pushes both opens). An with a null href drops the attribute and renders as inert text, so the non-link step is a plain anchor with no link semantics. --%> @if(complete)@template.lievit.icon(name = "check", size = "1rem")@else${number}@endif ${label} @if(!description.isEmpty()) ${description} @endif @if(!last) @template.lievit.separator() @endif

<%-- Copyright 2026 Francesco Bilotta Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"). lievit-ui wizard/footer partial -- the navigation bar at the bottom of a wizard (the Filament Wizard footer actions): a left cluster (PREVIOUS / CANCEL) and a right cluster (NEXT or, on the last step, SUBMIT). Server-first, no JS. TIER: STATIC presentational -> JTE partial. Which buttons show is server-owned: PREVIOUS shows off the first step (showPrevious), SUBMIT replaces NEXT on the last step (showSubmit). Each button is a real @endif @if(showCancel) @endif @if(showSubmit) @else @endif
